Title: Admin login with valid credentials
Preconditions: App deployed at https://byoc-rosy.vercel.app, admin account exists
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to https://byoc-rosy.vercel.app/login. Step 2: Enter email admin@exargen.com. Step 3: Enter password Admin123!. Step 4: Click Sign In button. Step 5: Verify redirect to /dashboard.
Expected Result: User is authenticated and redirected to the dashboard. Welcome message or user avatar shows Platform Administrator role.

Title: Analyst login with valid credentials
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to https://byoc-rosy.vercel.app/login. Step 2: Enter email analyst@exargen.com. Step 3: Enter password Analyst123!. Step 4: Click Sign In button. Step 5: Verify redirect to /dashboard.
Expected Result: User is authenticated and redirected to the dashboard. Role displayed is Security Analyst.

Title: Auditor login with valid credentials
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to https://byoc-rosy.vercel.app/login. Step 2: Enter email auditor@exargen.com. Step 3: Enter password Auditor123!. Step 4: Click Sign In button. Step 5: Verify redirect to /dashboard.
Expected Result: User is authenticated and redirected to the dashboard. Role displayed is Auditor.

Title: Viewer login with valid credentials
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to https://byoc-rosy.vercel.app/login. Step 2: Enter email viewer@exargen.com. Step 3: Enter password Viewer123!. Step 4: Click Sign In button. Step 5: Verify redirect to /dashboard.
Expected Result: User is authenticated and redirected to the dashboard. Role displayed is Viewer.

Title: Org Admin login with valid credentials
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to https://byoc-rosy.vercel.app/login. Step 2: Enter email orgadmin@exargen.com. Step 3: Enter password OrgAdmin123!. Step 4: Click Sign In button. Step 5: Verify redirect to /dashboard.
Expected Result: User is authenticated and redirected to the dashboard. Role displayed is Organization Administrator.

Title: Login with invalid password
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to https://byoc-rosy.vercel.app/login. Step 2: Enter email admin@exargen.com. Step 3: Enter password WrongPassword123!. Step 4: Click Sign In button. Step 5: Verify error message is displayed.
Expected Result: Login fails with an error message such as Invalid credentials. User remains on the login page.

Title: Login with non-existent email
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to https://byoc-rosy.vercel.app/login. Step 2: Enter email nonexistent@exargen.com. Step 3: Enter password SomePassword123!. Step 4: Click Sign In button. Step 5: Verify error message is displayed.
Expected Result: Login fails with an error message. No information leakage about whether the email exists.

Title: Login with empty email field
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to https://byoc-rosy.vercel.app/login. Step 2: Leave email field empty. Step 3: Enter password Admin123!. Step 4: Click Sign In button. Step 5: Verify validation error is displayed.
Expected Result: Form validation prevents submission or displays a required field error for email.

Title: Login with empty password field
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to https://byoc-rosy.vercel.app/login. Step 2: Enter email admin@exargen.com. Step 3: Leave password field empty. Step 4: Click Sign In button. Step 5: Verify validation error is displayed.
Expected Result: Form validation prevents submission or displays a required field error for password.

Title: Logout functionality
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com with password Admin123!. Step 2: Locate the user menu or logout button. Step 3: Click Logout. Step 4: Verify redirect to /login page. Step 5: Attempt to navigate to /dashboard directly. Step 6: Verify redirect back to /login.
Expected Result: User is logged out and redirected to login page. Accessing protected routes redirects to login.

Title: JWT cookie is created on login
Steps: Step 1: Open browser dev tools (F12) and go to Application > Cookies. Step 2: Clear all cookies. Step 3: Navigate to https://byoc-rosy.vercel.app/login. Step 4: Log in as admin@exargen.com with password Admin123!. Step 5: Check Application > Cookies for the auth token cookie. Step 6: Verify the cookie has the httpOnly flag set.
Expected Result: A JWT auth cookie is created with httpOnly flag set to true preventing client-side JavaScript access.

Title: JWT cookie is removed on logout
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with dev tools open
Steps: Step 1: Verify auth token cookie exists in Application > Cookies. Step 2: Click Logout. Step 3: Check Application > Cookies again. Step 4: Verify the auth token cookie has been removed or expired.
Expected Result: The JWT auth cookie is cleared from the browser after logout.

Title: Expired JWT prevents access
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Open dev tools and manually modify the auth cookie expiry to a past date. Step 3: Refresh the /dashboard page. Step 4: Verify the user is redirected to /login.
Expected Result: An expired JWT token does not grant access. User is redirected to the login page.

Title: Tampered JWT is rejected
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Open dev tools and copy the auth cookie value. Step 3: Modify one character in the cookie value. Step 4: Replace the cookie with the tampered value. Step 5: Refresh the /dashboard page. Step 6: Verify the user is redirected to /login.
Expected Result: A tampered JWT token is rejected by the server. User is redirected to login.

Title: Session persists across page refresh
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /dashboard. Step 3: Press F5 to refresh the page. Step 4: Verify the user remains logged in on the dashboard. Step 5: Navigate to another protected page like /assets. Step 6: Verify the user remains authenticated.
Expected Result: Session persists across page refreshes and navigation to different protected routes.

Title: Concurrent sessions from different browsers
Steps: Step 1: Open Chrome and log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Open Firefox and log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 3: Verify both sessions are active and can access /dashboard. Step 4: Log out from Chrome. Step 5: Verify Firefox session remains active.
Expected Result: Multiple concurrent sessions are supported. Logging out of one does not affect others.

Title: Platform Admin has all 46 capabilities
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/roles. Step 3: Click on the Platform Administrator role. Step 4: Verify all 46 capabilities are assigned. Step 5: Verify capabilities span all 9 modules: Dashboard Scans Assets Risk Compliance Reports AI SIEM Administration.
Expected Result: Platform Administrator role shows all 46 capabilities checked across all 9 modules.

Title: Viewer has only 4 capabilities
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/roles. Step 3: Click on the Viewer role. Step 4: Count the assigned capabilities. Step 5: Verify only dash.view risk.view report.view and report.export are assigned. Step 6: Verify no other capabilities are checked.
Expected Result: Viewer role has exactly 4 capabilities: dash.view risk.view report.view report.export.

Title: Auditor has exactly 19 capabilities
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/roles. Step 3: Click on the Auditor role. Step 4: Count all assigned capabilities. Step 5: Verify the following 19 are present: dash.view scan.view scan.export scan.policy.view asset.view asset.export risk.view compliance.view compliance.export report.view report.export ai.view siem.view admin.audit.view admin.audit.export admin.user.view admin.role.view admin.sso.view admin.scim.view.
Expected Result: Auditor role has exactly 19 read-only and export capabilities with no write or manage capabilities.

Title: Security Analyst has exactly 28 capabilities
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/roles. Step 3: Click on the Security Analyst role. Step 4: Count all assigned capabilities. Step 5: Verify 28 capabilities including dash.view dash.customize scan.view scan.create scan.execute scan.schedule scan.export scan.policy.view asset.view asset.edit asset.import asset.export risk.view risk.override compliance.view compliance.assess compliance.export report.view report.create report.schedule report.export ai.view ai.approve.standard siem.view siem.acknowledge siem.escalate admin.apikey.manage admin.audit.view.
Expected Result: Security Analyst role has exactly 28 capabilities covering operational SOC duties.

Title: Seven built-in roles exist
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/roles. Step 3: Count the number of built-in roles listed. Step 4: Verify the following 7 roles exist: Platform Administrator Organization Administrator Security Analyst Auditor Viewer Remediation User API Service Account.
Expected Result: All 7 built-in roles are listed on the roles page.

Title: Org Admin has all capabilities except billing
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/roles. Step 3: Click on the Organization Administrator role. Step 4: Verify all capabilities are checked except admin.billing.manage. Step 5: Confirm admin.billing.manage is explicitly denied or unchecked.
Expected Result: Organization Administrator has 45 capabilities (all 46 minus admin.billing.manage).

Title: Remediation User has 6 view-only capabilities
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/roles. Step 3: Click on the Remediation User role. Step 4: Verify exactly 6 capabilities: dash.view scan.view asset.view risk.view compliance.view report.view. Step 5: Verify no edit create or manage capabilities are assigned.
Expected Result: Remediation User has exactly 6 view-only capabilities.

Title: API Service Account has 8 capabilities
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/roles. Step 3: Click on the API Service Account role. Step 4: Verify exactly 8 capabilities: scan.view scan.create scan.execute asset.view asset.import report.view report.export siem.view. Step 5: Verify no admin or AI capabilities are assigned.
Expected Result: API Service Account has exactly 8 capabilities for machine-to-machine access.

Title: Admin can access scan creation page
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /scans. Step 3: Verify the scans page loads successfully. Step 4: Look for a New Scan or Create Scan button. Step 5: Click the button. Step 6: Verify the scan creation form or dialog appears.
Expected Result: Platform Admin can access the scans page and create new scans (scan.create capability).

Title: Viewer cannot access scan creation
Steps: Step 1: Log in as viewer@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /scans. Step 3: Verify the page shows access denied or the navigation link is hidden. Step 4: Try navigating directly to /scans in the URL bar. Step 5: Verify no scan creation button is visible or the page returns 403.
Expected Result: Viewer cannot access the scans module. The page shows access denied or the nav link is hidden.

Title: Viewer cannot access assets page
Steps: Step 1: Log in as viewer@exargen.com. Step 2: Check the sidebar navigation for an Assets link. Step 3: If present click it otherwise navigate directly to /assets. Step 4: Verify the page shows access denied or redirects away.
Expected Result: Viewer without asset.view capability cannot access the assets page.

Title: Analyst can view and acknowledge SIEM events
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /siem. Step 3: Verify SIEM events are displayed. Step 4: Find an unacknowledged event. Step 5: Click the acknowledge button. Step 6: Verify the event status changes to acknowledged.
Expected Result: Security Analyst can view SIEM events (siem.view) and acknowledge alerts (siem.acknowledge).

Title: Analyst cannot approve critical AI actions
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /ai-actions. Step 3: Verify AI actions are visible (ai.view). Step 4: Look for a critical-risk AI action. Step 5: Verify the approve button for critical actions is disabled or hidden. Step 6: Confirm only standard approve is available.
Expected Result: Security Analyst can view AI actions but cannot approve critical ones (lacks ai.approve.critical).

Title: Auditor can view audit log
Steps: Step 1: Log in as auditor@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /audit. Step 3: Verify the audit log page loads successfully. Step 4: Verify audit entries are displayed with timestamps actions and users. Step 5: Verify an export button is visible (admin.audit.export).
Expected Result: Auditor can access and view the full audit trail and export audit logs.

Title: Auditor can export audit logs
Steps: Step 1: Log in as auditor@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /audit. Step 3: Click the Export button. Step 4: Verify the export dialog or download initiates. Step 5: Verify the exported file contains audit data.
Expected Result: Auditor with admin.audit.export capability can export audit log data.

Title: Auditor cannot create or modify scans
Steps: Step 1: Log in as auditor@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /scans. Step 3: Verify scans are visible (scan.view). Step 4: Verify no Create Scan or New Scan button is present. Step 5: Click on an existing scan. Step 6: Verify no edit or execute controls are available.
Expected Result: Auditor can view scans but cannot create edit or execute them.

Title: Analyst can create scans
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /scans. Step 3: Click the New Scan or Create Scan button. Step 4: Fill in scan target details. Step 5: Submit the scan creation form. Step 6: Verify the new scan appears in the scans list.
Expected Result: Security Analyst with scan.create capability can create new scans.

Title: Analyst can execute scans
Preconditions: Logged in as analyst@exargen.com with an existing scan
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /scans. Step 3: Find a scan in pending or ready state. Step 4: Click the Run or Execute button. Step 5: Verify the scan status changes to running or in-progress. Step 6: Monitor scan progress updates.
Expected Result: Security Analyst with scan.execute capability can launch scan jobs.

Title: Analyst can export scan results
Preconditions: Logged in as analyst@exargen.com with completed scans
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /scans. Step 3: Click on a completed scan. Step 4: Look for an Export button. Step 5: Click Export and select a format. Step 6: Verify the download initiates.
Expected Result: Security Analyst with scan.export capability can export scan results.

Title: Admin can manage users
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/users. Step 3: Verify the user list is displayed. Step 4: Verify an Invite User button is present. Step 5: Click on a user to view their profile. Step 6: Verify edit controls are available for the user.
Expected Result: Platform Admin with admin.user.manage capability can view and manage users.

Title: Auditor can view users but not manage
Steps: Step 1: Log in as auditor@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/users. Step 3: Verify the user list is displayed (admin.user.view). Step 4: Verify no Invite User or Add User button is visible. Step 5: Click on a user. Step 6: Verify no edit or delete controls are available.
Expected Result: Auditor can view users but cannot invite edit or delete users.

Title: Viewer cannot access user management
Steps: Step 1: Log in as viewer@exargen.com. Step 2: Check sidebar for a Settings or Users link. Step 3: If link is hidden navigate directly to /settings/users. Step 4: Verify access is denied or the page is not accessible.
Expected Result: Viewer without admin.user.view cannot access the user management page.

Title: Admin can access compliance module
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /compliance. Step 3: Verify compliance frameworks are displayed. Step 4: Click on a framework. Step 5: Verify controls are listed. Step 6: Verify manage controls are available (compliance.manage).
Expected Result: Platform Admin can access all compliance features including framework management.

Title: Viewer can access dashboard only
Steps: Step 1: Log in as viewer@exargen.com. Step 2: Verify the dashboard loads with KPI widgets. Step 3: Check sidebar navigation links. Step 4: Verify only Dashboard Reports and Risk-related links are visible. Step 5: Verify links for Scans Assets SIEM AI Actions Audit and Settings are hidden.
Expected Result: Viewer sees only dashboard reports and risk sections in the navigation matching their 4 capabilities.

Title: Gate component hides unauthorized UI elements
Steps: Step 1: Log in as viewer@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /dashboard. Step 3: Inspect the page for any scan-related buttons or links. Step 4: Verify no Create Scan Execute Scan or Scan Management UI elements are visible. Step 5: Open browser dev tools and search DOM for hidden scan elements. Step 6: Verify Gate component correctly hides gated elements.
Expected Result: Frontend Gate component hides UI elements the user lacks capabilities for. No hidden but accessible elements in DOM.

Title: Gate shows alternative message for denied access
Steps: Step 1: Log in as viewer@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to a page that uses GateMessage component for unauthorized content. Step 3: Verify a friendly access denied or insufficient permissions message is shown. Step 4: Verify the message does not expose which specific capability is missing.
Expected Result: GateMessage component displays a user-friendly message when access is denied rather than showing nothing.

Title: Sidebar navigation reflects role capabilities
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com. Step 2: Examine the sidebar navigation links. Step 3: Verify Dashboard Scans Assets Compliance Reports AI Actions SIEM links are visible. Step 4: Verify Audit link is visible (admin.audit.view). Step 5: Verify Settings > SSO and SCIM links are hidden (no admin.sso.view or admin.scim.view).
Expected Result: Sidebar navigation shows only links the user has capabilities to access.

Title: Unauthorized API request returns 403
Preconditions: Logged in as viewer@exargen.com with dev tools open
Steps: Step 1: Log in as viewer@exargen.com. Step 2: Open dev tools Network tab. Step 3: Navigate to /dashboard. Step 4: In dev tools console execute fetch /api/scans with POST method. Step 5: Examine the response status code. Step 6: Verify the response is 403 Forbidden.
Expected Result: API endpoints return 403 Forbidden when the user lacks the required capability.

Title: Unauthenticated API request returns 401
Steps: Step 1: Open a new incognito/private browser window. Step 2: Open dev tools console. Step 3: Execute fetch to https://byoc-rosy.vercel.app/api/scans. Step 4: Examine the response status code. Step 5: Verify the response is 401 Unauthorized.
Expected Result: API endpoints return 401 Unauthorized when no valid session exists.

Title: Admin API request succeeds
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with dev tools open
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Open dev tools Network tab. Step 3: Navigate to /scans. Step 4: Observe the API requests in the Network tab. Step 5: Verify the scan list API call returns 200 OK. Step 6: Verify scan data is returned in the response.
Expected Result: API endpoints return 200 OK and valid data when the user has the required capability.

Title: Auditor API request for scan creation returns 403
Preconditions: Logged in as auditor@exargen.com with dev tools open
Steps: Step 1: Log in as auditor@exargen.com. Step 2: Open dev tools console. Step 3: Execute a POST fetch to /api/scans with a scan creation payload. Step 4: Examine the response status code. Step 5: Verify the response is 403 Forbidden. Step 6: Verify no scan is created.
Expected Result: Auditor cannot create scans via API. The server returns 403 for POST /api/scans.

Title: Admin can configure AI behavior
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /ai-actions. Step 3: Look for AI configuration or settings controls. Step 4: Verify autonomy level or auto-approval settings are accessible. Step 5: Verify the ai.configure capability grants access to these controls.
Expected Result: Platform Admin with ai.configure capability can access AI behavior configuration.

Title: Admin can manage roles
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/roles. Step 3: Verify a Create Role button is present. Step 4: Click Create Role. Step 5: Fill in role name and select some capabilities. Step 6: Save the role. Step 7: Verify the new role appears in the roles list.
Expected Result: Platform Admin with admin.role.manage capability can create custom roles.

Title: Analyst can view reports and create new ones
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /reports. Step 3: Verify existing reports are listed (report.view). Step 4: Look for a Generate Report button. Step 5: Click it and verify the report generation form appears (report.create). Step 6: Verify report scheduling option is available (report.schedule).
Expected Result: Security Analyst can view create and schedule reports per their capabilities.

Title: Auditor can view compliance and export
Steps: Step 1: Log in as auditor@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /compliance. Step 3: Verify compliance frameworks and controls are displayed (compliance.view). Step 4: Look for an Export button. Step 5: Verify export functionality is accessible (compliance.export). Step 6: Verify no Assess or Manage controls are visible.
Expected Result: Auditor can view and export compliance data but cannot assess controls or manage frameworks.

Title: Deny-wins conflict resolution
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with a test user having conflicting roles
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/users. Step 3: Find or create a test user with two roles where one grants a capability and another denies it. Step 4: Log in as the test user. Step 5: Attempt to access the denied capability. Step 6: Verify the deny takes precedence and access is blocked.
Expected Result: When a user has multiple roles with conflicting capability grants and denials the deny always wins.

Title: Multi-role cumulative assignment
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/users. Step 3: Select a user and assign both Viewer and Security Analyst roles. Step 4: Log in as that user. Step 5: Verify the user has the combined capabilities of both roles. Step 6: Verify access to both dashboard-only features and analyst features.
Expected Result: Multiple roles are cumulative. A user with Viewer + Security Analyst gets the union of both capability sets.

Title: Account lockout after failed attempts
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to https://byoc-rosy.vercel.app/login. Step 2: Enter email admin@exargen.com. Step 3: Enter an incorrect password and click Sign In. Step 4: Repeat step 3 four more times (5 total failed attempts). Step 5: Attempt to log in with the correct password Admin123!. Step 6: Verify the account is locked and login is denied even with correct credentials.
Expected Result: Account is locked after the configured number of failed login attempts. Correct credentials are rejected during lockout.

Title: Protected routes redirect to login when unauthenticated
Steps: Step 1: Open a new incognito browser window. Step 2: Navigate directly to https://byoc-rosy.vercel.app/dashboard. Step 3: Verify redirect to /login. Step 4: Navigate to https://byoc-rosy.vercel.app/scans. Step 5: Verify redirect to /login. Step 6: Navigate to https://byoc-rosy.vercel.app/settings/users. Step 7: Verify redirect to /login.
Expected Result: All protected routes redirect unauthenticated users to the login page.

Title: Create a new tag as admin
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/tags or the tag management section. Step 3: Click Create Tag or Add Tag button. Step 4: Enter tag name such as environment:production. Step 5: Save the tag. Step 6: Verify the new tag appears in the tag list.
Expected Result: Admin can create a new tag and it appears in the tag list.

Title: Edit an existing tag
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with at least one tag existing
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to the tag management section. Step 3: Click on an existing tag to edit it. Step 4: Modify the tag name or value. Step 5: Save the changes. Step 6: Verify the updated tag name is displayed.
Expected Result: Admin can edit an existing tag and changes are persisted.

Title: Delete a tag
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with a deletable tag existing
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to the tag management section. Step 3: Find a tag that is not in use. Step 4: Click the delete button for that tag. Step 5: Confirm the deletion in the dialog. Step 6: Verify the tag is removed from the list.
Expected Result: Admin can delete an unused tag and it is removed from the system.

Title: View all tags in the system
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to the tag management section. Step 3: Verify a list of all tags is displayed. Step 4: Verify each tag shows its name and usage count. Step 5: Verify tags are organized or searchable.
Expected Result: All tags in the system are listed with their metadata.

Title: Prevent duplicate tag creation
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with tag environment:production existing
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to tag management. Step 3: Click Create Tag. Step 4: Enter the same name as an existing tag. Step 5: Attempt to save. Step 6: Verify an error message about duplicate tag is shown.
Expected Result: System prevents creation of duplicate tags and shows an appropriate error.

Title: Analyst cannot create tags
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to the tag management section. Step 3: Verify no Create Tag button is visible. Step 4: Attempt to access tag creation via direct URL if available. Step 5: Verify access is denied.
Expected Result: Security Analyst without tag.manage capability cannot create tags.

Title: Auditor cannot modify tags
Steps: Step 1: Log in as auditor@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to the tag management section. Step 3: Verify tags are visible if tag.view is granted. Step 4: Verify no edit or delete buttons are available. Step 5: Verify the auditor has read-only access to tag information.
Expected Result: Auditor can view tags but cannot create edit or delete them.

Title: Apply a tag to an asset
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with assets and tags existing
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /assets. Step 3: Click on an existing asset. Step 4: Find the tag assignment section. Step 5: Select a tag like environment:production. Step 6: Save the tag assignment. Step 7: Verify the tag appears on the asset.
Expected Result: Admin can apply tags to assets and the tag is visually displayed on the asset.

Title: Remove a tag from an asset
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with a tagged asset
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /assets. Step 3: Click on a tagged asset. Step 4: Find the tag on the asset. Step 5: Click the remove or X button on the tag. Step 6: Save the changes. Step 7: Verify the tag is no longer on the asset.
Expected Result: Admin can remove tags from assets and the change is persisted.

Title: Apply multiple tags to a single asset
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with multiple tags available
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /assets. Step 3: Click on an asset. Step 4: Add tag environment:production. Step 5: Add tag team:security. Step 6: Add tag region:us-east. Step 7: Save and verify all three tags are displayed on the asset.
Expected Result: Multiple tags can be applied to a single asset simultaneously.

Title: Filter assets by tag
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with tagged assets
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /assets. Step 3: Locate the tag filter or search functionality. Step 4: Select or enter a tag name to filter by. Step 5: Verify only assets with the selected tag are displayed. Step 6: Clear the filter and verify all assets are shown again.
Expected Result: Asset list can be filtered by tags showing only matching assets.

Title: Create a new scope as admin
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to the scope management section under settings. Step 3: Click Create Scope. Step 4: Enter scope name such as US Production. Step 5: Select tags to include in the scope like environment:production and region:us-east. Step 6: Save the scope. Step 7: Verify the new scope appears in the scope list.
Expected Result: Admin can create a new scope with tag-based rules and it appears in the scope list.

Title: Edit an existing scope
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with at least one scope existing
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to scope management. Step 3: Click on an existing scope. Step 4: Modify the scope name or change included tags. Step 5: Save the changes. Step 6: Verify the updated scope details are displayed.
Expected Result: Admin can edit scope configuration and changes are persisted.

Title: Delete a scope
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with a deletable scope
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to scope management. Step 3: Find a scope that can be deleted. Step 4: Click delete on the scope. Step 5: Confirm the deletion. Step 6: Verify the scope is removed from the list.
Expected Result: Admin can delete a scope and it is removed from the system.

Title: View all scopes
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to scope management. Step 3: Verify all scopes are listed. Step 4: Verify each scope shows its name and included tags. Step 5: Verify the Global scope is present.
Expected Result: All scopes including the Global scope are listed with their tag associations.

Title: Assign a scope to a user
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/users. Step 3: Click on a user such as analyst@exargen.com. Step 4: Find the scope assignment section. Step 5: Select a scope like US Production. Step 6: Save the assignment. Step 7: Verify the scope is shown on the user profile.
Expected Result: Admin can assign a scope to a user limiting their data visibility.

Title: Remove scope from a user
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with a user having an assigned scope
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/users. Step 3: Click on the user with an assigned scope. Step 4: Remove the scope assignment. Step 5: Save the changes. Step 6: Verify the scope is no longer assigned to the user.
Expected Result: Admin can remove a scope assignment from a user restoring broader access.

Title: Assign multiple scopes to a user
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with multiple scopes created
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/users. Step 3: Click on a user. Step 4: Assign scope US Production. Step 5: Also assign scope EU Compliance. Step 6: Save the changes. Step 7: Verify both scopes appear on the user profile.
Expected Result: Multiple scopes can be assigned to a single user. The user sees data from the union of all assigned scopes.

Title: Scoped user sees only in-scope assets
Preconditions: analyst@exargen.com assigned to a scope with tag environment:production
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /assets. Step 3: Verify only assets tagged with environment:production are displayed. Step 4: Count the assets shown. Step 5: Log out and log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 6: Navigate to /assets and verify more assets are visible than the analyst saw.
Expected Result: Scoped user sees only assets that match their assigned scope tags.

Title: Scoped user sees only in-scope scans
Preconditions: analyst@exargen.com assigned to a restricted scope
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com with a restricted scope. Step 2: Navigate to /scans. Step 3: Verify only scans related to in-scope assets are shown. Step 4: Verify scan results only contain findings for in-scope assets. Step 5: Compare with admin view to confirm filtering is working.
Expected Result: Scoped user sees only scans and findings related to their scoped assets.

Title: Scoped user sees only in-scope SIEM events
Preconditions: analyst@exargen.com assigned to a restricted scope
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com with a restricted scope. Step 2: Navigate to /siem. Step 3: Verify only SIEM events related to in-scope assets are displayed. Step 4: Compare the event count with an admin view. Step 5: Verify no out-of-scope events leak through.
Expected Result: SIEM events are filtered by the user scope showing only events for in-scope assets.

Title: Scoped user dashboard shows only in-scope data
Preconditions: analyst@exargen.com assigned to a restricted scope
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com with a restricted scope. Step 2: Navigate to /dashboard. Step 3: Verify KPI widgets show data only for in-scope assets. Step 4: Verify charts and counts reflect scoped data. Step 5: Compare with admin dashboard totals to confirm filtering.
Expected Result: Dashboard KPIs and widgets reflect only the data within the user assigned scope.

Title: Compliance view respects scope
Preconditions: analyst@exargen.com assigned to a restricted scope
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com with a restricted scope. Step 2: Navigate to /compliance. Step 3: Verify compliance data shown is relevant to in-scope assets only. Step 4: Check assessment counts and control statuses. Step 5: Verify no out-of-scope compliance data is visible.
Expected Result: Compliance module data is filtered by user scope.

Title: Global scope grants access to all data
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Verify the admin has Global scope or no scope restriction. Step 3: Navigate to /assets. Step 4: Verify all assets across all tags are visible. Step 5: Navigate to /scans and verify all scans are visible. Step 6: Navigate to /siem and verify all events are visible.
Expected Result: Global scope provides unrestricted access to all tenant data across all tags.

Title: Platform Admin bypasses scope restrictions
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com even if a scope is assigned
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /assets. Step 3: Verify all assets are visible regardless of any scope assignment. Step 4: Navigate to /scans and verify all scans visible. Step 5: Verify Platform Admin always sees all data. Step 6: Verify the admin bypass is consistent across all modules.
Expected Result: Platform Administrator always bypasses scope filtering and sees all data.

Title: Org Admin bypasses scope restrictions
Steps: Step 1: Log in as orgadmin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /assets. Step 3: Verify all assets are visible regardless of scope. Step 4: Navigate to /scans. Step 5: Verify all scans are visible. Step 6: Verify Org Admin has unrestricted data access like Platform Admin.
Expected Result: Organization Administrator bypasses scope restrictions and sees all data.

Title: Create an auto-tag rule with equals operator
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to the auto-tag rules management section. Step 3: Click Create Rule. Step 4: Set the field to asset name. Step 5: Set operator to equals. Step 6: Set value to web-server-01. Step 7: Set the tag to apply as team:web. Step 8: Save the rule.
Expected Result: Auto-tag rule with equals operator is created successfully.

Title: Auto-tag rule with contains operator
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to auto-tag rules. Step 3: Click Create Rule. Step 4: Set field to asset name. Step 5: Set operator to contains. Step 6: Set value to prod. Step 7: Set tag to environment:production. Step 8: Save the rule. Step 9: Create an asset with prod in its name and verify the tag is auto-applied.
Expected Result: Auto-tag rule with contains operator matches assets containing the substring and applies the tag.

Title: Auto-tag rule with starts_with operator
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to auto-tag rules. Step 3: Create a rule with operator starts_with and value db-. Step 4: Set tag to type:database. Step 5: Save the rule. Step 6: Create an asset named db-primary-01. Step 7: Verify the type:database tag is automatically applied.
Expected Result: Auto-tag rule with starts_with operator matches assets whose name starts with the value.

Title: Auto-tag rule with ends_with operator
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to auto-tag rules. Step 3: Create a rule with operator ends_with and value -staging. Step 4: Set tag to environment:staging. Step 5: Save the rule. Step 6: Create an asset named api-server-staging. Step 7: Verify the environment:staging tag is automatically applied.
Expected Result: Auto-tag rule with ends_with operator matches assets whose name ends with the value.

Title: Auto-tag rule with regex operator
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to auto-tag rules. Step 3: Create a rule with operator regex and value ^web-[0-9]+$. Step 4: Set tag to type:webserver. Step 5: Save the rule. Step 6: Create an asset named web-42. Step 7: Verify the type:webserver tag is automatically applied. Step 8: Create an asset named web-alpha and verify the tag is NOT applied.
Expected Result: Auto-tag rule with regex operator matches assets against regular expression patterns.

Title: Auto-tag rule with not_equals operator
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to auto-tag rules. Step 3: Create a rule with operator not_equals and value internal-only. Step 4: Set tag to visibility:external. Step 5: Save the rule. Step 6: Create an asset named public-api. Step 7: Verify the visibility:external tag is applied. Step 8: Create an asset named internal-only and verify the tag is NOT applied.
Expected Result: Auto-tag rule with not_equals operator applies tag to all assets except those matching the exact value.

Title: Edit an auto-tag rule
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with an existing auto-tag rule
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to auto-tag rules. Step 3: Click on an existing rule. Step 4: Change the operator from equals to contains. Step 5: Update the value. Step 6: Save the changes. Step 7: Verify the rule is updated.
Expected Result: Admin can edit auto-tag rules and changes take effect.

Title: Delete an auto-tag rule
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with an existing auto-tag rule
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to auto-tag rules. Step 3: Find a rule to delete. Step 4: Click the delete button. Step 5: Confirm the deletion. Step 6: Verify the rule is removed. Step 7: Verify previously auto-tagged assets retain their tags.
Expected Result: Auto-tag rule is deleted but previously applied tags remain on assets.

Title: Auto-tag applies to newly created assets
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with an active auto-tag rule
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Verify an auto-tag rule exists with contains operator for prod. Step 3: Navigate to /assets. Step 4: Create a new asset named prod-server-new. Step 5: Save the asset. Step 6: View the asset details. Step 7: Verify the auto-tag has been applied automatically.
Expected Result: Auto-tag rules are evaluated on new asset creation and matching tags are applied immediately.

Title: Preview scope shows matching assets
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to scope management. Step 3: Create or edit a scope. Step 4: Select tags for the scope. Step 5: Click Preview or view the scope preview panel. Step 6: Verify a list or count of matching assets is displayed. Step 7: Verify the preview matches the actual tagged assets.
Expected Result: Scope preview shows which assets will be included in the scope before saving.

Title: Preview updates dynamically when tags change
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com editing a scope
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to scope edit. Step 3: Note the current preview count. Step 4: Add an additional tag to the scope. Step 5: Verify the preview count updates to reflect more assets. Step 6: Remove a tag. Step 7: Verify the preview count decreases accordingly.
Expected Result: Scope preview updates in real-time as tags are added or removed from the scope definition.

Title: API enforces scope on asset listing
Preconditions: analyst@exargen.com with a restricted scope and dev tools open
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com with a restricted scope. Step 2: Open dev tools Network tab. Step 3: Navigate to /assets. Step 4: Inspect the API response for the asset list endpoint. Step 5: Verify the response contains only assets matching the scope tags. Step 6: Verify no out-of-scope asset IDs appear in the response.
Expected Result: API response for asset listing is filtered server-side by the user scope.

Title: API enforces scope on scan results
Preconditions: analyst@exargen.com with a restricted scope and dev tools open
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com with a restricted scope. Step 2: Open dev tools Network tab. Step 3: Navigate to /scans and click on a scan. Step 4: Inspect the API response for scan findings. Step 5: Verify findings are only for in-scope assets. Step 6: Verify no out-of-scope asset findings leak in the API response.
Expected Result: Scan result API responses are filtered server-side by user scope.

Title: API prevents accessing out-of-scope asset by ID
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com and note an asset ID that is outside the analyst scope. Step 2: Log out and log in as analyst@exargen.com. Step 3: Open dev tools console. Step 4: Execute a fetch to /api/assets/{out-of-scope-id}. Step 5: Verify the response is 403 or 404. Step 6: Verify no data for the out-of-scope asset is returned.
Expected Result: API prevents direct access to out-of-scope assets by ID returning 403 or 404.

Title: API enforces scope on SIEM event listing
Preconditions: analyst@exargen.com with a restricted scope and dev tools open
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com with a restricted scope. Step 2: Open dev tools Network tab. Step 3: Navigate to /siem. Step 4: Inspect the API response for SIEM events. Step 5: Verify only events related to in-scope assets are returned. Step 6: Compare with admin API response to confirm filtering.
Expected Result: SIEM event API responses are filtered server-side by user scope.

Title: Report data respects scope
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com with a restricted scope. Step 2: Navigate to /reports. Step 3: Generate a new report. Step 4: Verify the report data includes only in-scope assets and findings. Step 5: Export the report. Step 6: Verify exported data contains only scoped items.
Expected Result: Reports generated by a scoped user contain only data within their assigned scope.

Title: AI actions respect scope
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com with a restricted scope. Step 2: Navigate to /ai-actions. Step 3: Verify AI action recommendations shown are only for in-scope assets. Step 4: Verify no out-of-scope asset references appear in AI action details.
Expected Result: AI actions and recommendations are filtered to show only in-scope items.

Title: Risk scores respect scope
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com with a restricted scope. Step 2: Navigate to the risk scoring section. Step 3: Verify risk scores are shown only for in-scope assets. Step 4: Verify aggregate risk metrics reflect only scoped data. Step 5: Compare with admin view to confirm filtering.
Expected Result: Risk scores and metrics are filtered by user scope.

Title: Tag with special characters
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to tag management. Step 3: Click Create Tag. Step 4: Enter a tag name with special characters like team:dev-ops_2024. Step 5: Save the tag. Step 6: Verify the tag is created with special characters preserved.
Expected Result: Tags can contain special characters like hyphens underscores and numbers.

Title: Scope with no tags shows empty preview
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to scope management. Step 3: Start creating a new scope. Step 4: Do not select any tags. Step 5: Check the scope preview. Step 6: Verify the preview shows zero matching assets or a warning message.
Expected Result: A scope with no tags selected shows an empty preview with zero matching assets.

Title: Viewer with scope sees filtered dashboard
Preconditions: Viewer assigned to a restricted scope
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Assign a restricted scope to viewer@exargen.com. Step 3: Log out and log in as viewer@exargen.com. Step 4: Navigate to /dashboard. Step 5: Verify dashboard KPIs show data only for in-scope assets. Step 6: Verify risk scores and report summaries reflect scoped data only.
Expected Result: Even Viewer role with limited capabilities respects scope filtering on the dashboard.

Title: Reassigning scope updates data visibility immediately
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com and verify data from scope A is visible on /assets. Step 2: Log in as admin@exargen.com in another browser. Step 3: Change analyst scope from scope A to scope B. Step 4: Back in the analyst browser refresh the /assets page. Step 5: Verify the asset list now shows scope B assets instead of scope A. Step 6: Verify old scope A assets are no longer visible.
Expected Result: Scope changes take effect immediately upon next request without requiring re-login.

Title: Scoped user cannot see audit entries for out-of-scope items
Preconditions: analyst@exargen.com with a restricted scope and admin.audit.view
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com with a restricted scope. Step 2: Navigate to /audit. Step 3: Verify audit log entries are displayed. Step 4: Verify entries only pertain to in-scope resources or the user own actions. Step 5: Verify no audit entries for out-of-scope assets appear.
Expected Result: Audit log view respects scope filtering showing only relevant entries.

Title: Bulk asset export respects scope
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com with a restricted scope. Step 2: Navigate to /assets. Step 3: Click Export All or similar bulk export button. Step 4: Download the export file. Step 5: Open the file and count assets. Step 6: Verify only in-scope assets are included in the export.
Expected Result: Bulk export operations respect scope and only include in-scope data.

Title: Multiple auto-tag rules can match same asset
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with two auto-tag rules
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Create rule 1: name contains prod applies tag environment:production. Step 3: Create rule 2: name contains web applies tag type:webserver. Step 4: Create an asset named prod-web-01. Step 5: Verify both tags environment:production and type:webserver are applied. Step 6: View asset details to confirm both tags.
Expected Result: Multiple auto-tag rules can match and apply multiple tags to the same asset.

Title: Scope with multiple tags uses AND logic
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Create a scope with two tags: environment:production AND team:security. Step 3: Check scope preview. Step 4: Verify only assets that have BOTH tags are shown. Step 5: Verify assets with only one of the two tags are excluded.
Expected Result: Scope with multiple tags filters using AND logic requiring all tags to match.

Title: Empty scope shows no data to assigned user
Preconditions: analyst@exargen.com assigned to a scope with no matching assets
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Create a scope with a tag that no assets have. Step 3: Assign this scope to analyst@exargen.com. Step 4: Log in as analyst@exargen.com. Step 5: Navigate to /assets. Step 6: Verify zero assets are displayed. Step 7: Navigate to /dashboard and verify KPIs show zero counts.
Expected Result: A scope with no matching assets shows empty data views to the assigned user.

Title: Deleting a tag used in a scope shows warning
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with a tag used in a scope
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to tag management. Step 3: Attempt to delete a tag that is used in one or more scopes. Step 4: Verify a warning or confirmation dialog appears. Step 5: Verify the warning mentions the scope impact. Step 6: Cancel the deletion.
Expected Result: System warns when deleting a tag that is referenced by scopes to prevent orphaned scope definitions.

Title: API dashboard endpoint respects scope
Preconditions: analyst@exargen.com with a restricted scope and dev tools open
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com with a restricted scope. Step 2: Open dev tools Network tab. Step 3: Navigate to /dashboard. Step 4: Inspect the API responses for dashboard data endpoints. Step 5: Verify aggregate counts and metrics in the response are scoped. Step 6: Compare response values with admin dashboard API to confirm filtering.
Expected Result: Dashboard API endpoints return scoped aggregate data matching the user assigned scope.

Title: User list page loads with all tenant users
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com at https://byoc-rosy.vercel.app/login. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/users. Step 3: Verify the user list page loads without errors. Step 4: Verify all 5 seeded users are listed (admin analyst auditor viewer orgadmin). Step 5: Verify each row shows name email role and status columns.
Expected Result: User list page displays all tenant users with name email role and status columns.

Title: Search users by name
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/users. Step 2: Locate the search input field. Step 3: Type Platform Admin into the search field. Step 4: Verify the list filters to show only the admin user. Step 5: Clear the search field. Step 6: Verify all users are shown again.
Expected Result: Search by name filters the user list to show only matching users.

Title: Search users by email
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/users. Step 2: Locate the search input field. Step 3: Type analyst@exargen.com into the search field. Step 4: Verify only the analyst user is displayed. Step 5: Clear the search. Step 6: Type exargen.com and verify all users appear.
Expected Result: Search by email filters the user list correctly.

Title: Filter users by role
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/users. Step 2: Locate the role filter dropdown. Step 3: Select Security Analyst from the filter. Step 4: Verify only analyst@exargen.com is displayed. Step 5: Change filter to Viewer. Step 6: Verify only viewer@exargen.com is displayed. Step 7: Clear the filter and verify all users appear.
Expected Result: Role filter restricts the user list to users with the selected role.

Title: Filter users by status active
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/users. Step 2: Locate the status filter dropdown. Step 3: Select Active from the filter. Step 4: Verify only active users are displayed. Step 5: Verify suspended or invited users are not shown.
Expected Result: Status filter shows only users with active status.

Title: Filter users by status suspended
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with at least one suspended user
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/users. Step 2: Suspend a user if none are suspended. Step 3: Select Suspended from the status filter. Step 4: Verify only suspended users are shown. Step 5: Verify active users are hidden. Step 6: Clear filter and verify all users return.
Expected Result: Status filter shows only suspended users when selected.

Title: Filter users by status invited
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with a pending invitation
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/users. Step 2: Invite a new user if no pending invitations exist. Step 3: Select Invited from the status filter. Step 4: Verify only users with invited status are shown. Step 5: Verify active and suspended users are hidden.
Expected Result: Status filter shows only users with pending invitation status.

Title: View user detail page
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/users. Step 2: Click on analyst@exargen.com row. Step 3: Verify user detail view opens. Step 4: Verify name is displayed. Step 5: Verify email analyst@exargen.com is shown. Step 6: Verify assigned roles are listed. Step 7: Verify assigned scopes are listed. Step 8: Verify last login timestamp is shown.
Expected Result: User detail view shows complete user profile with name email roles scopes and last login.

Title: User detail shows correct role badges
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/users. Step 2: Click on admin@exargen.com row. Step 3: Verify Platform Administrator role badge is displayed. Step 4: Go back and click on auditor@exargen.com. Step 5: Verify Auditor role badge is displayed. Step 6: Verify role badges match assigned roles.
Expected Result: User detail displays correct role badges for each user.

Title: User detail shows scope assignments
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with scoped users
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/users. Step 2: Click on a user who has a scope assigned. Step 3: Verify the scope section shows the assigned scope name. Step 4: Verify scope tags are displayed. Step 5: Click on a user with Global scope and verify Global is shown.
Expected Result: User detail view correctly displays all assigned scopes for the user.

Title: Suspend an active user
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/users. Step 2: Click on viewer@exargen.com. Step 3: Click the Suspend button. Step 4: Confirm the suspension in the dialog. Step 5: Verify the user status changes to Suspended. Step 6: Verify a success notification is shown.
Expected Result: Admin can suspend an active user and status changes to Suspended.

Title: Suspended user cannot login
Preconditions: viewer@exargen.com has been suspended by admin
Steps: Step 1: Log out from admin session. Step 2: Navigate to https://byoc-rosy.vercel.app/login. Step 3: Enter email viewer@exargen.com. Step 4: Enter password Viewer123!. Step 5: Click Sign In. Step 6: Verify login fails with an account suspended or disabled error message. Step 7: Verify user remains on the login page.
Expected Result: Suspended user cannot authenticate and sees an appropriate error message.

Title: Reactivate a suspended user
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with viewer suspended
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/users. Step 2: Click on the suspended viewer@exargen.com. Step 3: Click the Reactivate button. Step 4: Confirm the reactivation. Step 5: Verify the status changes back to Active. Step 6: Verify a success notification is shown.
Expected Result: Admin can reactivate a suspended user and their status returns to Active.

Title: Reactivated user can login again
Preconditions: viewer@exargen.com has been reactivated by admin
Steps: Step 1: Log out from admin session. Step 2: Navigate to https://byoc-rosy.vercel.app/login. Step 3: Enter email viewer@exargen.com. Step 4: Enter password Viewer123!. Step 5: Click Sign In. Step 6: Verify login succeeds. Step 7: Verify redirect to /dashboard.
Expected Result: Reactivated user can log in successfully with their original credentials.

Title: Invite a new user with role selection
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/users. Step 2: Click the Invite User button. Step 3: Enter email newuser@exargen.com in the email field. Step 4: Select Security Analyst role from the role dropdown. Step 5: Click Send Invitation. Step 6: Verify a success message is shown. Step 7: Verify the invited user appears in the user list with Invited status.
Expected Result: Admin can invite a new user by email with a selected role and the invitation appears in the list.

Title: Accept invitation flow sets password
Steps: Step 1: Retrieve the invitation link from the email or API response. Step 2: Open the invitation link in a new browser window. Step 3: Verify the /accept-invitation page loads. Step 4: Enter a new password meeting complexity requirements. Step 5: Confirm the password. Step 6: Click Accept or Create Account. Step 7: Verify account is created and user can log in.
Expected Result: New user can accept invitation by setting a password and then logging in.

Title: Resend invitation email
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with a pending invitation
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/users. Step 2: Find the user with Invited status. Step 3: Click on the user row or find the Resend option. Step 4: Click Resend Invitation. Step 5: Verify a success message confirms the invitation was resent. Step 6: Verify the invitation status remains Invited.
Expected Result: Admin can resend an invitation email to a pending user.

Title: Revoke pending invitation
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with a pending invitation
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/users. Step 2: Find the user with Invited status. Step 3: Click on the user row. Step 4: Click Revoke Invitation. Step 5: Confirm the revocation. Step 6: Verify the user is removed from the list or status updates. Step 7: Verify the old invitation link no longer works.
Expected Result: Admin can revoke a pending invitation rendering the invitation link invalid.

Title: Invite user with Viewer role
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/users. Step 2: Click Invite User. Step 3: Enter email viewertest@exargen.com. Step 4: Select Viewer role. Step 5: Click Send Invitation. Step 6: Verify invitation is sent. Step 7: Verify the invited user shows Viewer role in the list.
Expected Result: Invitation can be sent with Viewer role assignment.

Title: Invite user with multiple roles
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/users. Step 2: Click Invite User. Step 3: Enter email multiuser@exargen.com. Step 4: Select Security Analyst role. Step 5: Also select Auditor role if multi-select is available. Step 6: Click Send Invitation. Step 7: Verify invitation shows both roles.
Expected Result: User can be invited with multiple role assignments.

Title: Role list displays all built-in roles
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/roles. Step 2: Verify the role list page loads. Step 3: Count the roles displayed. Step 4: Verify Platform Administrator is listed. Step 5: Verify Organization Administrator Security Analyst Auditor Viewer Remediation User and API Service Account are all present. Step 6: Verify 7 built-in roles total.
Expected Result: Role list page shows all 7 built-in roles.

Title: Role list shows custom roles alongside built-in
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with at least one custom role
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/roles. Step 2: Verify built-in roles are displayed. Step 3: Verify any custom roles are also listed. Step 4: Verify custom roles are visually distinguished from built-in roles. Step 5: Verify the total count includes both built-in and custom roles.
Expected Result: Custom roles appear alongside built-in roles in the role list.

Title: Create a custom role with capabilities
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/roles. Step 2: Click Create Role button. Step 3: Enter role name SOC Tier 1. Step 4: Enter description First line SOC analyst with limited capabilities. Step 5: Select capabilities: dash.view scan.view asset.view siem.view siem.acknowledge. Step 6: Click Save. Step 7: Verify the new SOC Tier 1 role appears in the role list.
Expected Result: Custom role is created with selected capabilities and appears in the role list.

Title: Create role with empty name shows validation error
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/roles. Step 2: Click Create Role. Step 3: Leave the name field empty. Step 4: Select at least one capability. Step 5: Click Save. Step 6: Verify a validation error appears for the required name field.
Expected Result: Role creation requires a name and shows validation error when missing.

Title: Create role with duplicate name shows error
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with existing custom role SOC Tier 1
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/roles. Step 2: Click Create Role. Step 3: Enter role name SOC Tier 1 (already exists). Step 4: Select some capabilities. Step 5: Click Save. Step 6: Verify an error message about duplicate role name is shown.
Expected Result: System prevents creation of roles with duplicate names.

Title: Edit custom role to add capabilities
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with custom role SOC Tier 1
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/roles. Step 2: Click on the SOC Tier 1 custom role. Step 3: Click Edit. Step 4: Add ai.view capability. Step 5: Click Save. Step 6: Verify the role now shows the additional capability. Step 7: Verify existing capabilities are still present.
Expected Result: Custom role can be edited to add new capabilities while preserving existing ones.

Title: Edit custom role to remove capabilities
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with custom role SOC Tier 1 having ai.view
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/roles. Step 2: Click on the SOC Tier 1 custom role. Step 3: Click Edit. Step 4: Uncheck the ai.view capability. Step 5: Click Save. Step 6: Verify ai.view is no longer assigned to the role. Step 7: Verify other capabilities remain unchanged.
Expected Result: Custom role can be edited to remove capabilities.

Title: Built-in role cannot be edited

Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with multiple sessions active
Steps: Step 1: Log in from three different browsers. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/sessions in browser 1. Step 3: Click Revoke All Other Sessions. Step 4: Confirm the action. Step 5: Verify only the current session remains in the list. Step 6: Verify browsers 2 and 3 are forced to re-login. Step 7: Verify the current session in browser 1 remains active.
Expected Result: Revoke all other sessions terminates all sessions except the current one.

Title: Revoked session cannot access protected routes
Preconditions: Session for browser 2 has been revoked
Steps: Step 1: In browser 2 where the session was revoked attempt to navigate to /dashboard. Step 2: Verify the user is redirected to /login. Step 3: Attempt to access /api/scans via dev tools fetch. Step 4: Verify the API returns 401 Unauthorized. Step 5: Verify no cached data is accessible.
Expected Result: A revoked session token is immediately invalidated and cannot access any protected resources.

Title: Session cleanup of expired sessions
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with dev tools open
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Open dev tools console. Step 3: Execute POST fetch to /api/auth/sessions/cleanup. Step 4: Verify the response is 200 OK. Step 5: Verify the response indicates how many expired sessions were cleaned. Step 6: Navigate to /settings/sessions and verify only valid sessions remain.
Expected Result: Session cleanup endpoint removes expired sessions from the database.

Title: Account locks after 5 failed login attempts
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /login. Step 2: Enter admin@exargen.com with wrong password Attempt1. Step 3: Click Sign In and note the error. Step 4: Repeat with wrong passwords Attempt2 Attempt3 Attempt4 Attempt5. Step 5: On the 6th attempt enter correct password Admin123!. Step 6: Verify login is denied even with correct password. Step 7: Verify the error message indicates account lockout.
Expected Result: Account is locked after 5 consecutive failed login attempts blocking even correct credentials.

Title: Locked account shows appropriate error message
Steps: Step 1: Attempt to log in to the locked admin@exargen.com account. Step 2: Enter correct password Admin123!. Step 3: Click Sign In. Step 4: Verify the error message indicates the account is locked. Step 5: Verify the message mentions a timeout period or contact administrator. Step 6: Verify no information leakage about remaining lockout time.
Expected Result: Locked account displays a clear lockout message without revealing security details.

Title: Account auto-unlocks after timeout period
Steps: Step 1: Wait for the lockout timeout period to expire (15 minutes). Step 2: Navigate to /login. Step 3: Enter admin@exargen.com with correct password Admin123!. Step 4: Click Sign In. Step 5: Verify login succeeds. Step 6: Verify redirect to /dashboard.
Expected Result: Account automatically unlocks after the configured timeout period allowing login with correct credentials.

Title: Admin can manually reset lockout
Preconditions: Logged in as orgadmin@exargen.com; admin@exargen.com is locked
Steps: Step 1: Log in as orgadmin@exargen.com. Step 2: Open dev tools console. Step 3: Get the user ID for admin@exargen.com. Step 4: Execute POST to /api/admin/users/{id}/reset-lockout. Step 5: Verify 200 response. Step 6: Log out and attempt to log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 7: Verify login succeeds with Admin123!.
Expected Result: Admin lockout reset endpoint immediately clears the lockout allowing the user to log in.

Title: Create an API key
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/security. Step 2: Find the API Keys section. Step 3: Click Create API Key. Step 4: Enter name Automation Key. Step 5: Select an expiry date 30 days from now. Step 6: Click Create. Step 7: Verify the API key value is displayed once. Step 8: Copy and save the key value.
Expected Result: API key is created with name and expiry and the key value is shown only once upon creation.

Title: API key list shows created keys
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with at least one API key
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/security. Step 2: Find the API Keys section. Step 3: Verify the created API key Automation Key is listed. Step 4: Verify the key shows its name. Step 5: Verify the expiry date is displayed. Step 6: Verify the actual key value is masked or hidden (not shown in the list).
Expected Result: API key list page shows key names and expiry dates but masks the actual key values.

Title: API key shown only once after creation
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/security. Step 2: Create a new API key. Step 3: Verify the key value is displayed in a dialog or banner. Step 4: Close the dialog or navigate away. Step 5: Return to the API keys list. Step 6: Verify the full key value is no longer visible anywhere.
Expected Result: API key secret is displayed only once at creation time and cannot be retrieved afterwards.

Title: API key authenticates on API endpoints
Steps: Step 1: Open a new incognito browser. Step 2: Open dev tools console. Step 3: Execute a GET fetch to /api/scans with the API key in the Authorization header as Bearer {key}. Step 4: Verify the response is 200 OK. Step 5: Verify scan data is returned. Step 6: Verify the request is authenticated via the API key.
Expected Result: API key can be used as a Bearer token to authenticate API requests.

Title: Revoke an API key
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with an API key to revoke
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/security. Step 2: Find the API key to revoke in the list. Step 3: Click the Revoke button. Step 4: Confirm the revocation. Step 5: Verify the key is marked as revoked or removed from the list. Step 6: Verify a success notification is shown.
Expected Result: API key can be revoked by the admin removing its authentication capability.

Title: Revoked API key returns 401
Steps: Step 1: Take the revoked API key value. Step 2: Open dev tools console. Step 3: Execute a GET fetch to /api/scans with the revoked key in Authorization header. Step 4: Verify the response is 401 Unauthorized. Step 5: Verify no data is returned. Step 6: Verify the error message indicates invalid or revoked key.
Expected Result: A revoked API key is immediately rejected with 401 on all API endpoints.

Title: Expired API key returns 401
Preconditions: An API key with a past expiry date exists
Steps: Step 1: Create an API key with a very short expiry or modify expiry to past date. Step 2: Wait for the key to expire. Step 3: Execute a GET fetch to /api/scans with the expired key. Step 4: Verify the response is 401 Unauthorized. Step 5: Verify the error indicates the key is expired.
Expected Result: An expired API key is rejected with 401 Unauthorized.

Title: Content-Security-Policy header present
Steps: Step 1: Open dev tools Network tab. Step 2: Navigate to https://byoc-rosy.vercel.app/login. Step 3: Click on the document request. Step 4: Examine the response headers. Step 5: Verify Content-Security-Policy header is present. Step 6: Verify the CSP policy restricts script sources.
Expected Result: Content-Security-Policy response header is present with appropriate directives.

Title: X-Frame-Options header present
Steps: Step 1: Open dev tools Network tab. Step 2: Navigate to https://byoc-rosy.vercel.app. Step 3: Click on the document request. Step 4: Examine response headers. Step 5: Verify X-Frame-Options is set to DENY or SAMEORIGIN. Step 6: Verify the header prevents clickjacking attacks.
Expected Result: X-Frame-Options header is present preventing the page from being embedded in iframes.

Title: X-Content-Type-Options header present
Steps: Step 1: Open dev tools Network tab. Step 2: Navigate to https://byoc-rosy.vercel.app. Step 3: Click on the document request. Step 4: Examine response headers. Step 5: Verify X-Content-Type-Options is set to nosniff.
Expected Result: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff header prevents MIME type sniffing.

Title: Strict-Transport-Security header present
Steps: Step 1: Open dev tools Network tab. Step 2: Navigate to https://byoc-rosy.vercel.app. Step 3: Click on the document request. Step 4: Examine response headers. Step 5: Verify Strict-Transport-Security header is present. Step 6: Verify it includes max-age directive.
Expected Result: HSTS header enforces HTTPS connections with appropriate max-age.

Title: Referrer-Policy header present
Steps: Step 1: Open dev tools Network tab. Step 2: Navigate to https://byoc-rosy.vercel.app. Step 3: Click on the document request. Step 4: Examine response headers. Step 5: Verify Referrer-Policy is set to strict-origin-when-cross-origin or similar restrictive policy.
Expected Result: Referrer-Policy header is present restricting referrer information leakage.

Title: All five security headers present in single response
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Open dev tools Network tab. Step 3: Navigate to /dashboard. Step 4: Click on the document request. Step 5: Verify Content-Security-Policy is present. Step 6: Verify X-Frame-Options is present. Step 7: Verify X-Content-Type-Options is present. Step 8: Verify Strict-Transport-Security and Referrer-Policy are present.
Expected Result: All five security headers are present on protected pages in a single HTTP response.

Title: CSRF protection on POST endpoints
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with dev tools open
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Open dev tools console. Step 3: Execute a POST fetch to /api/scans without CSRF token. Step 4: Verify the request succeeds if session cookie is present (SameSite cookie protection). Step 5: Open a different origin page. Step 6: Attempt cross-origin POST to /api/scans and verify it is blocked.
Expected Result: POST endpoints are protected against CSRF attacks via SameSite cookies or CSRF tokens.

Title: CSRF protection on PATCH endpoints
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with dev tools open
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Execute a PATCH request to /api/users/{id} from same origin. Step 3: Verify the request succeeds. Step 4: Verify the auth cookie has SameSite attribute set. Step 5: Verify cross-origin PATCH requests would be blocked.
Expected Result: PATCH endpoints are CSRF protected via cookie SameSite policy.

Title: CSRF protection on DELETE endpoints
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with dev tools open
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Execute a DELETE request to a valid endpoint from same origin. Step 3: Verify it processes correctly. Step 4: Check the auth cookie SameSite attribute. Step 5: Verify cross-origin DELETE requests are blocked by browser SameSite enforcement.
Expected Result: DELETE endpoints are protected against cross-origin CSRF attacks.

Title: Login rate limiting triggers 429
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /login. Step 2: Rapidly submit login attempts with wrong credentials. Step 3: Continue submitting at least 10-20 rapid attempts. Step 4: Verify eventually a 429 Too Many Requests response is returned. Step 5: Verify the error message indicates rate limiting. Step 6: Wait for the rate limit window to reset.
Expected Result: Login endpoint returns 429 Too Many Requests after exceeding the rate limit threshold.

Title: Rate limit headers present on login response
Steps: Step 1: Open dev tools Network tab. Step 2: Submit a login attempt. Step 3: Click on the login request in the Network tab. Step 4: Examine response headers. Step 5: Look for X-RateLimit-Limit header. Step 6: Look for X-RateLimit-Remaining header. Step 7: Verify the remaining count decreases with each attempt.
Expected Result: Rate limit headers (X-RateLimit-Limit and X-RateLimit-Remaining) are present on login responses.

Title: Rate limit resets after window expires
Steps: Step 1: Trigger the rate limit by making rapid login attempts. Step 2: Note the time when 429 was received. Step 3: Wait for the rate limit window to expire (typically 1-15 minutes). Step 4: Attempt to log in again. Step 5: Verify the login attempt is processed normally. Step 6: Verify a 200 or 401 response (not 429).
Expected Result: Rate limiting resets after the configured time window allowing new requests.

Title: Password must meet complexity requirements
Steps: Step 1: Open an invitation acceptance link. Step 2: Enter a simple password like password. Step 3: Click submit. Step 4: Verify a validation error about password complexity is shown. Step 5: Enter a password with uppercase lowercase number and special char like Strong1!. Step 6: Verify the password is accepted.
Expected Result: Password complexity rules enforce minimum requirements including uppercase lowercase numbers and special characters.

Title: Weak password rejected with error message
Steps: Step 1: Open an invitation acceptance link. Step 2: Enter password 123456. Step 3: Attempt to submit. Step 4: Verify error message about weak password. Step 5: Enter password abc. Step 6: Verify error about minimum length. Step 7: Enter a strong password and verify acceptance.
Expected Result: Weak passwords are rejected with specific error messages about what requirements are not met.

Title: Password minimum length enforced
Steps: Step 1: Open an invitation acceptance link. Step 2: Enter a 5-character password A1b!c. Step 3: Attempt to submit. Step 4: Verify error about minimum length. Step 5: Enter an 8-character password A1b!cDe2. Step 6: Verify the password meets the length requirement.
Expected Result: Password must meet minimum length requirement (typically 8 characters).

Title: Scan execution recorded in audit log
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com after running a scan
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /scans and execute a scan. Step 3: Navigate to /settings/audit-log. Step 4: Search for scan-related entries. Step 5: Verify scan.execute or scan.create action is recorded. Step 6: Verify the actor and timestamp are correct.
Expected Result: Scan execution events are captured in the audit log.

Title: Invitation sent recorded in audit log
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com after sending invitation
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/users and invite a new user. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/audit-log. Step 3: Look for user.invite or invitation.send action. Step 4: Verify the entry shows the invited email. Step 5: Verify the actor is admin@exargen.com.
Expected Result: User invitation events are recorded in the audit log.

Title: API key creation recorded in audit log
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com after creating API key
Steps: Step 1: Create a new API key at /settings/security. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/audit-log. Step 3: Look for apikey.create action. Step 4: Verify the entry shows the key name. Step 5: Verify the actor is admin@exargen.com. Step 6: Verify the key value itself is NOT logged.
Expected Result: API key creation is audited but the key secret is never stored in the audit log.

Title: API key revocation recorded in audit log
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com after revoking API key
Steps: Step 1: Revoke an API key at /settings/security. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/audit-log. Step 3: Look for apikey.revoke action. Step 4: Verify the entry shows which key was revoked. Step 5: Verify the actor and timestamp.
Expected Result: API key revocation events are captured in the audit log.

Title: Session last used time updates on activity
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/sessions. Step 2: Note the last used timestamp for the current session. Step 3: Navigate to /dashboard. Step 4: Navigate to /scans. Step 5: Return to /settings/sessions. Step 6: Verify the last used timestamp has updated to a more recent time.
Expected Result: Session last used timestamp is updated as the user navigates and makes requests.

Title: Multiple sessions from same user visible
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com from two browsers
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com from Chrome. Step 2: Log in as admin@exargen.com from Firefox. Step 3: Navigate to /settings/sessions in Chrome. Step 4: Verify at least two sessions are listed. Step 5: Verify each session shows different device or browser info. Step 6: Verify both sessions are marked as active.
Expected Result: Multiple concurrent sessions from the same user are individually tracked and displayed.

Title: Revoke session via API endpoint
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with dev tools open
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Get the session list from /api/auth/sessions. Step 3: Pick a session ID to revoke (not the current one). Step 4: Execute DELETE to /api/auth/sessions/{sessionId}. Step 5: Verify 200 response. Step 6: Verify the session no longer appears in the list.
Expected Result: Session can be revoked programmatically via the API endpoint.

Title: Revoke all sessions via API endpoint
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with dev tools open
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Open dev tools console. Step 3: Execute POST to /api/auth/sessions/revoke-all. Step 4: Verify 200 response. Step 5: Verify the response indicates sessions were revoked. Step 6: Refresh /settings/sessions and verify only the current session remains.
Expected Result: Revoke all sessions API endpoint terminates all sessions except the current one.

Title: Lockout counter resets on successful login
Preconditions: admin@exargen.com has 3 failed attempts but not locked
Steps: Step 1: Make 3 failed login attempts for admin@exargen.com. Step 2: On the 4th attempt enter correct password Admin123!. Step 3: Verify login succeeds. Step 4: Log out. Step 5: Make 3 more failed attempts. Step 6: On the 4th attempt enter correct password and verify login succeeds.
