Expected Result: Successful login resets the failed attempt counter to zero.

Title: Different accounts lock independently
Preconditions: Both admin and analyst accounts available
Steps: Step 1: Make 4 failed login attempts for admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Log in as analyst@exargen.com with correct password. Step 3: Verify analyst login succeeds (not affected by admin attempts). Step 4: Make the 5th failed attempt for admin@exargen.com. Step 5: Verify admin is locked. Step 6: Verify analyst can still log in normally.
Expected Result: Account lockout is per-user and does not affect other accounts.

Title: API key has correct expiry enforcement
Steps: Step 1: Create an API key with a 1-day expiry. Step 2: Immediately test the key with a GET to /api/scans. Step 3: Verify 200 response. Step 4: Verify the key works within the expiry window. Step 5: Check the API key list shows the correct expiry date.
Expected Result: API key expiry date is stored and enforced correctly.

Title: API key without expiry works indefinitely
Preconditions: API key created without an expiry date if supported
Steps: Step 1: Create an API key without setting an expiry. Step 2: Test the key with a GET to /api/scans. Step 3: Verify 200 response. Step 4: Verify the key list shows no expiry or never for the key. Step 5: Verify the key continues to work.
Expected Result: API key without expiry remains valid until explicitly revoked.

Title: Non-admin cannot access audit log page
Steps: Step 1: Log in as viewer@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate directly to /settings/audit-log. Step 3: Verify access is denied or redirected. Step 4: Verify no audit data is displayed. Step 5: Verify the sidebar does not show an audit log link for viewer.
Expected Result: Users without admin.audit.view cannot access the audit log page.

Title: Non-admin cannot access session management
Steps: Step 1: Log in as viewer@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate directly to /settings/sessions. Step 3: Verify access is denied or redirected. Step 4: Verify no session data is displayed. Step 5: Check sidebar for hidden session management link.
Expected Result: Users without session management capability cannot access the sessions page.

Title: Non-admin cannot create API keys
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/security. Step 3: Verify the API key creation section is hidden or shows a permission message. Step 4: Attempt POST to /api/api-keys via dev tools. Step 5: Verify 403 response.
Expected Result: Users without admin.apikey.manage cannot create API keys.

Title: Auditor can view audit log
Steps: Step 1: Log in as auditor@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/audit-log. Step 3: Verify the page loads successfully. Step 4: Verify audit entries are displayed. Step 5: Verify the Export button is visible (admin.audit.export). Step 6: Verify no delete or modify controls are present.
Expected Result: Auditor with admin.audit.view and admin.audit.export can view and export the audit log.

Title: Auditor can export but not modify audit log
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/audit-log. Step 2: Verify Export button is clickable. Step 3: Click Export and verify download initiates. Step 4: Verify no Edit or Delete buttons exist on audit entries. Step 5: Verify no bulk modify or purge options are available.
Expected Result: Auditor has read-only and export access to the audit log with no modification capability.

Title: Access denied events logged in audit
Steps: Step 1: Log in as viewer@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/users (should be denied). Step 3: Log out and log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 4: Navigate to /settings/audit-log. Step 5: Look for an access denied entry for viewer. Step 6: Verify the denied action and actor are recorded.
Expected Result: Authorization denials are captured in the audit log with the denied action and user.

Title: Each audit entry has unique hash
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/audit-log. Step 2: View details of the first audit entry and note its hash. Step 3: View details of the second entry and note its hash. Step 4: Verify the two hashes are different. Step 5: View a third entry. Step 6: Verify all three hashes are unique.
Expected Result: Every audit log entry has a unique SHA-256 hash ensuring individual integrity.

Title: Hash chain links entries sequentially
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/audit-log. Step 2: View details of entry N. Step 3: Note its previous hash field. Step 4: View details of entry N-1. Step 5: Verify entry N previous hash matches entry N-1 current hash. Step 6: Verify this chain relationship is consistent.
Expected Result: Audit log entries are cryptographically chained with each entry referencing the previous entry hash.

Title: API key name is required
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/security. Step 2: Click Create API Key. Step 3: Leave the name field empty. Step 4: Click Create. Step 5: Verify a validation error appears for the required name field. Step 6: Enter a name and verify creation succeeds.
Expected Result: API key creation requires a name and shows validation error when missing.

Title: API responses include security headers
Preconditions: Dev tools open; logged in as admin@exargen.com
Steps: Step 1: Open dev tools Network tab. Step 2: Navigate to /dashboard to trigger API calls. Step 3: Click on an API request (e.g. /api/scans). Step 4: Examine response headers. Step 5: Verify security headers are present on API responses too. Step 6: Verify X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff is on API responses.
Expected Result: Security headers are applied to API responses not just page responses.

Title: Audit log entries are immutable
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with dev tools open
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/audit-log. Step 2: View an audit entry. Step 3: Verify no Edit or Delete buttons exist on the entry. Step 4: Attempt to send a PATCH or DELETE request to /api/audit-log via dev tools. Step 5: Verify the server rejects modification attempts. Step 6: Verify the audit log is append-only.
Expected Result: Audit log entries are immutable and cannot be modified or deleted through the UI or API.

Title: Session management shows correct session count
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/sessions. Step 2: Count the number of active sessions displayed. Step 3: Log in from an additional browser. Step 4: Refresh /settings/sessions in the first browser. Step 5: Verify the session count increased by one. Step 6: Verify the new session appears in the list.
Expected Result: Session management page accurately reflects the current number of active sessions.

Title: Lockout status visible in user detail
Steps: Step 1: Lock out a user account by making 5+ failed attempts. Step 2: Log in as orgadmin@exargen.com. Step 3: Navigate to /settings/users. Step 4: Click on the locked user. Step 5: Verify the user detail shows a locked or lockout status indicator. Step 6: Verify a reset lockout option is available.
Expected Result: Admin can see the lockout status of a user in their detail view.

Title: Audit export includes hash chain data
Steps: Step 1: Export the audit log as CSV. Step 2: Open the CSV file. Step 3: Verify a hash column exists. Step 4: Verify a previousHash column exists. Step 5: Verify the hash values are 64-character hex strings (SHA-256). Step 6: Verify the chain can be verified from the exported data.
Expected Result: Exported audit CSV includes hash chain data enabling offline integrity verification.

Title: Rate limiting on API endpoints
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with dev tools open
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Open dev tools console. Step 3: Execute a rapid loop of 50+ GET requests to /api/scans. Step 4: Check response headers for rate limit information. Step 5: Verify rate limiting is applied if thresholds are configured. Step 6: Verify X-RateLimit headers are present.
Expected Result: API endpoints implement rate limiting to prevent abuse.

Title: Bulk actions recorded individually in audit log
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com after performing a bulk action
Steps: Step 1: Perform a bulk action such as revoking all sessions. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/audit-log. Step 3: Verify the bulk action is recorded. Step 4: Verify individual entries exist for each affected item if applicable. Step 5: Verify the actor and timestamp are correct for all entries.
Expected Result: Bulk actions produce individual audit entries for each affected resource enabling granular tracking.

Title: Compliance page loads with all 5 frameworks
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /compliance. Step 3: Verify the compliance page loads without errors. Step 4: Verify 5 frameworks are listed: GDPR PCI DSS HIPAA CIS v8.1 NIST CSF 2.0. Step 5: Verify the total control count across all frameworks sums to 73.
Expected Result: Compliance page displays all 5 frameworks with a combined total of 73 controls (GDPR 10 + PCI DSS 12 + HIPAA 11 + CIS v8.1 18 + NIST CSF 2.0 22).

Title: Overall compliance score displays correctly
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance. Step 2: Locate the overall compliance score widget or header. Step 3: Verify the score is displayed as a percentage. Step 4: Verify the score reflects the aggregate status across all 73 controls. Step 5: Verify the score updates reflect compliant partial and non-compliant counts.
Expected Result: Overall compliance score is displayed as a percentage reflecting the weighted status of all assessed controls.

Title: GDPR framework shows correct control count and score
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance. Step 2: Locate the GDPR framework card. Step 3: Verify it shows 10 controls. Step 4: Verify the framework score percentage is displayed. Step 5: Verify compliant partial non-compliant and pending counts are shown. Step 6: Verify the counts sum to 10.
Expected Result: GDPR framework displays 10 controls with accurate score percentage and status breakdown.

Title: PCI DSS framework shows correct control count and score
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance. Step 2: Locate the PCI DSS framework card. Step 3: Verify it shows 12 controls. Step 4: Verify the framework score percentage is displayed. Step 5: Verify status breakdown counts sum to 12.
Expected Result: PCI DSS framework displays 12 controls with accurate score percentage and status breakdown.

Title: HIPAA framework shows correct control count and score
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance. Step 2: Locate the HIPAA framework card. Step 3: Verify it shows 11 controls. Step 4: Verify the framework score percentage is displayed. Step 5: Verify status breakdown counts sum to 11.
Expected Result: HIPAA framework displays 11 controls with accurate score percentage and status breakdown.

Title: CIS v8.1 framework shows correct control count and score
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance. Step 2: Locate the CIS v8.1 framework card. Step 3: Verify it shows 18 controls. Step 4: Verify the framework score percentage is displayed. Step 5: Verify status breakdown counts sum to 18.
Expected Result: CIS v8.1 framework displays 18 controls with accurate score percentage and status breakdown.

Title: NIST CSF 2.0 framework shows correct control count and score
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance. Step 2: Locate the NIST CSF 2.0 framework card. Step 3: Verify it shows 22 controls. Step 4: Verify the framework score percentage is displayed. Step 5: Verify status breakdown counts sum to 22.
Expected Result: NIST CSF 2.0 framework displays 22 controls with accurate score percentage and status breakdown.

Title: Click framework to expand and view controls
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance. Step 2: Click on the GDPR framework card. Step 3: Verify the framework expands to show a list of controls. Step 4: Verify each control shows its ID title and current status. Step 5: Count the controls and verify 10 are displayed.
Expected Result: Clicking a framework card expands it to reveal its full list of controls with IDs titles and statuses.

Title: Control list shows correct status badges
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at /compliance with controls visible
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance and expand a framework. Step 2: Identify a compliant control and verify it shows a green badge. Step 3: Identify a partial control and verify it shows a yellow badge. Step 4: Identify a non-compliant control and verify it shows a red badge. Step 5: Identify a not-assessed control and verify it shows a gray badge.
Expected Result: Control status badges use correct colors: green for compliant yellow for partial red for non-compliant gray for not-assessed.

Title: CIS v8.1 category filter tabs display correctly
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at /compliance with CIS expanded
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance. Step 2: Expand the CIS v8.1 framework. Step 3: Verify category filter tabs are displayed above the controls list. Step 4: Verify tabs include categories such as Asset Management Endpoint Security Data Protection. Step 5: Click a category tab. Step 6: Verify only controls in that category are shown.
Expected Result: CIS v8.1 framework shows category filter tabs that correctly filter the displayed controls.

Title: NIST CSF 2.0 category filter tabs display correctly
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at /compliance with NIST expanded
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance. Step 2: Expand the NIST CSF 2.0 framework. Step 3: Verify category filter tabs are displayed. Step 4: Verify tabs match NIST function categories. Step 5: Click each tab and verify controls filter appropriately.
Expected Result: NIST CSF 2.0 framework shows category filter tabs that correctly filter controls by function.

Title: PCI DSS category filter tabs display correctly
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at /compliance with PCI expanded
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance. Step 2: Expand the PCI DSS framework. Step 3: Verify category filter tabs are displayed. Step 4: Click a category tab. Step 5: Verify only controls in that category are shown. Step 6: Click All tab and verify all 12 controls display.
Expected Result: PCI DSS framework shows category filter tabs that correctly filter the displayed controls.

Title: Click control status badge to open assessment dialog
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at /compliance with controls visible
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance and expand a framework. Step 2: Find a control in the list. Step 3: Click the status badge on that control. Step 4: Verify an assessment dialog opens. Step 5: Verify the dialog shows the control ID and title. Step 6: Verify the dialog contains assessment form fields.
Expected Result: Clicking a control status badge opens the assessment dialog pre-populated with the control details.

Title: Assessment dialog allows selecting compliance status
Preconditions: Assessment dialog open for a control
Steps: Step 1: Open the assessment dialog for any control. Step 2: Locate the status selection dropdown or radio buttons. Step 3: Verify options include Compliant Partial Non-Compliant and Not Applicable. Step 4: Select Compliant. Step 5: Verify the selection is reflected in the form.
Expected Result: Assessment dialog provides status options: Compliant Partial Non-Compliant and Not Applicable.

Title: Assessment dialog accepts findings and notes
Preconditions: Assessment dialog open for a control
Steps: Step 1: Open the assessment dialog for any control. Step 2: Locate the findings or notes text area. Step 3: Enter detailed findings text such as Control verified through automated scanning. Step 4: Verify the text is accepted without character limit issues. Step 5: Verify the field supports multi-line input.
Expected Result: Assessment dialog findings text area accepts free-form notes about the assessment.

Title: Submit assessment and verify status updates immediately
Preconditions: Assessment dialog open with status and findings filled
Steps: Step 1: Open the assessment dialog for a not-assessed control. Step 2: Select status Compliant. Step 3: Enter findings text. Step 4: Click Submit or Save. Step 5: Verify the dialog closes. Step 6: Verify the control status badge immediately changes to green (compliant). Step 7: Verify the framework score updates.
Expected Result: Submitting an assessment immediately updates the control status badge and framework score.

Title: Assessment dialog allows adding evidence references
Preconditions: Assessment dialog open for a control
Steps: Step 1: Open the assessment dialog for any control. Step 2: Locate the evidence section. Step 3: Enter an evidence reference text such as SOC2 audit report 2024-Q4. Step 4: Click Add Evidence or press enter. Step 5: Verify the evidence reference appears as a tag or list item. Step 6: Verify the evidence count increments.
Expected Result: Evidence references can be added to assessments as text entries.

Title: Remove evidence reference from assessment
Preconditions: Assessment dialog open with at least one evidence reference
Steps: Step 1: Open the assessment dialog for a control with existing evidence. Step 2: Locate the evidence reference in the list. Step 3: Click the remove or X button on the evidence item. Step 4: Verify the evidence reference is removed. Step 5: Verify the evidence count decrements.
Expected Result: Evidence references can be removed from assessments.

Title: Evidence count badge displays on control
Preconditions: A control has been assessed with evidence references
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance and expand a framework. Step 2: Find a control that has been assessed with evidence. Step 3: Verify an evidence count badge is visible on the control row. Step 4: Verify the count matches the number of evidence references added.
Expected Result: Controls with evidence show a count badge indicating the number of evidence references.

Title: Assessment dialog allows entering remediation plan
Preconditions: Assessment dialog open for a non-compliant control
Steps: Step 1: Open the assessment dialog for a control. Step 2: Select status Non-Compliant. Step 3: Locate the remediation plan text area. Step 4: Enter a remediation plan such as Implement encryption at rest by Q2 2026. Step 5: Verify the text is accepted.
Expected Result: Assessment dialog allows entering a remediation plan for non-compliant controls.

Title: Due date input for remediation
Preconditions: Assessment dialog open with remediation plan
Steps: Step 1: Open the assessment dialog for a control. Step 2: Select status Non-Compliant. Step 3: Enter a remediation plan. Step 4: Locate the due date input field. Step 5: Select a future date. Step 6: Verify the date is displayed correctly in the form.
Expected Result: Assessment dialog provides a due date picker for remediation timelines.

Title: Change control from not-assessed to partial
Preconditions: A control currently in not-assessed status
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance and expand a framework. Step 2: Find a not-assessed control (gray badge). Step 3: Click the status badge to open assessment dialog. Step 4: Select Partial. Step 5: Enter findings. Step 6: Submit the assessment. Step 7: Verify the badge changes to yellow.
Expected Result: Control status changes from not-assessed (gray) to partial (yellow) after assessment submission.

Title: Change control from partial to compliant
Preconditions: A control currently in partial status
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance and expand a framework. Step 2: Find a partial control (yellow badge). Step 3: Click the status badge to open assessment dialog. Step 4: Select Compliant. Step 5: Enter updated findings. Step 6: Submit the assessment. Step 7: Verify the badge changes to green.
Expected Result: Control status changes from partial (yellow) to compliant (green) after re-assessment.

Title: Change control to non-compliant
Preconditions: A control currently in compliant or partial status
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance and expand a framework. Step 2: Find a compliant or partial control. Step 3: Click the status badge to open assessment dialog. Step 4: Select Non-Compliant. Step 5: Enter findings explaining the regression. Step 6: Submit the assessment. Step 7: Verify the badge changes to red.
Expected Result: Control status changes to non-compliant (red) after assessment with regression findings.

Title: Assessment history shows timeline for a control
Preconditions: A control that has been assessed at least twice
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance and expand a framework. Step 2: Find a control that has multiple assessments. Step 3: Expand the control or click a history button. Step 4: Verify a timeline of assessments is displayed. Step 5: Verify each entry shows assessor name status badge and relative time.
Expected Result: Assessment history displays a chronological timeline of all assessments for a control.

Title: History shows assessor name correctly
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance and expand a framework. Step 2: Open the assessment history for a control. Step 3: Verify each history entry shows the name of the user who performed the assessment. Step 4: Verify the assessor name matches the logged-in user who made each assessment.
Expected Result: Assessment history correctly displays the name of the assessor for each entry.

Title: History shows status badge for each assessment
Preconditions: A control with multiple assessment statuses in history
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance and expand a framework. Step 2: Open the assessment history for a control assessed multiple times. Step 3: Verify each history entry has a status badge. Step 4: Verify badges show the correct color for each historical status. Step 5: Verify the most recent status matches the current control badge.
Expected Result: Each assessment history entry shows the correct colored status badge matching its assessed status.

Title: History shows relative timestamps
Preconditions: A control with recent and older assessments
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance and expand a framework. Step 2: Open the assessment history for a control. Step 3: Verify timestamps display in relative format (e.g. 2 hours ago or 3 days ago). Step 4: Verify the most recent entry is at the top. Step 5: Verify older entries show progressively older timestamps.
Expected Result: Assessment history timestamps are displayed in relative time format ordered most recent first.

Title: History shows findings text
Preconditions: A control with assessments containing findings
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance and expand a framework. Step 2: Open the assessment history for a control. Step 3: Locate a history entry. Step 4: Verify the findings text from the assessment is displayed. Step 5: Verify evidence references are shown if they were attached.
Expected Result: Assessment history entries display the findings text and evidence references recorded during assessment.

Title: Multiple assessments on same control grow history
Preconditions: A control with one existing assessment
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance and expand a framework. Step 2: Open assessment history for a control and count entries. Step 3: Open the assessment dialog and submit a new assessment with different status. Step 4: Reopen the history. Step 5: Verify the entry count increased by one. Step 6: Verify the new entry appears at the top.
Expected Result: Each new assessment adds a new entry to the control assessment history.

Title: Export compliance report as CSV
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance. Step 2: Locate the Export button. Step 3: Click Export and select CSV format. Step 4: Verify a file download initiates. Step 5: Verify the Content-Disposition header indicates a CSV file. Step 6: Open the downloaded file and verify columns include Framework Version ControlID Title Category Status.
Expected Result: Compliance report exports as CSV with correct Content-Disposition header and expected columns.

Title: Export compliance report as JSON
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance. Step 2: Locate the Export button. Step 3: Click Export and select JSON format. Step 4: Verify a file download initiates. Step 5: Open the JSON file. Step 6: Verify the structure includes stats object with overall scores and a controls array with full control data.
Expected Result: Compliance report exports as JSON with stats and complete control data structure.

Title: CSV export contains all framework controls
Steps: Step 1: Export compliance report as CSV. Step 2: Open the CSV file. Step 3: Count the total data rows. Step 4: Verify the count matches 73 (total controls across all frameworks). Step 5: Verify each framework has the correct number of rows: GDPR 10 PCI DSS 12 HIPAA 11 CIS 18 NIST 22.
Expected Result: CSV export contains exactly 73 rows of control data matching all framework control counts.

Title: JSON export includes framework statistics
Steps: Step 1: Export compliance report as JSON. Step 2: Open and parse the JSON file. Step 3: Verify a stats object exists with overall compliance score. Step 4: Verify per-framework statistics are included. Step 5: Verify each framework stat includes compliant partial nonCompliant and notAssessed counts.
Expected Result: JSON export includes comprehensive statistics for overall and per-framework compliance.

Title: Framework filtering dropdown filters to single framework
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance. Step 2: Locate the framework filtering dropdown. Step 3: Select GDPR from the dropdown. Step 4: Verify only the GDPR framework is displayed. Step 5: Verify PCI DSS HIPAA CIS and NIST are hidden. Step 6: Verify the overall score recalculates for GDPR only.
Expected Result: Framework filtering dropdown shows only the selected framework and updates the score accordingly.

Title: Framework filter All Frameworks shows everything
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at /compliance with filter active
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance with a filter set to a single framework. Step 2: Open the framework filtering dropdown. Step 3: Select All Frameworks. Step 4: Verify all 5 frameworks are displayed again. Step 5: Verify the overall score reflects all frameworks.
Expected Result: Selecting All Frameworks in the dropdown restores the view of all 5 frameworks.

Title: Manage button opens framework management view
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance. Step 2: Locate the Manage button. Step 3: Click Manage. Step 4: Verify a framework management view or dialog opens. Step 5: Verify all 5 frameworks are listed with toggle switches. Step 6: Verify each framework shows its active/inactive status.
Expected Result: Manage button opens a view where administrators can toggle framework active status.

Title: Toggle framework isActive off hides from main view
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com in framework management
Steps: Step 1: Open framework management via the Manage button. Step 2: Toggle GDPR framework to inactive. Step 3: Confirm the action if prompted. Step 4: Close the management view. Step 5: Verify the GDPR framework is no longer visible in the main compliance view. Step 6: Verify only 4 frameworks remain visible.
Expected Result: Deactivating a framework hides it from the main compliance view.

Title: Reactivate framework and verify it reappears
Preconditions: GDPR framework was deactivated in previous test
Steps: Step 1: Open framework management via the Manage button. Step 2: Toggle GDPR framework back to active. Step 3: Confirm the action if prompted. Step 4: Close the management view. Step 5: Verify the GDPR framework reappears in the main compliance view. Step 6: Verify all 5 frameworks are visible again.
Expected Result: Reactivating a previously deactivated framework restores it to the main compliance view.

Title: Deactivated framework data is preserved
Preconditions: GDPR framework was deactivated and had assessments
Steps: Step 1: Assess several GDPR controls with statuses and findings. Step 2: Deactivate the GDPR framework. Step 3: Verify GDPR is hidden from the main view. Step 4: Reactivate the GDPR framework. Step 5: Verify all previous assessments statuses and history are preserved. Step 6: Verify the GDPR score matches its pre-deactivation score.
Expected Result: Deactivating and reactivating a framework preserves all assessment data and history.

Title: Viewer can see compliance page but cannot assess
Steps: Step 1: Log in as viewer@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /compliance. Step 3: Verify the compliance page loads and frameworks are visible. Step 4: Expand a framework to see controls. Step 5: Click a control status badge. Step 6: Verify the assessment dialog does not open or shows a permission denied message.
Expected Result: Viewer can view compliance data but the assessment functionality is blocked.

Title: Analyst with compliance.assess can update controls
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /compliance. Step 3: Expand a framework to see controls. Step 4: Click a control status badge. Step 5: Verify the assessment dialog opens. Step 6: Change the status and enter findings. Step 7: Submit and verify the update is saved.
Expected Result: Security Analyst with compliance.assess capability can open the assessment dialog and submit assessments.

Title: Export buttons require compliance.export capability
Steps: Step 1: Log in as viewer@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /compliance. Step 3: Look for Export CSV and Export JSON buttons. Step 4: Verify the export buttons are hidden or disabled for viewer. Step 5: Log out and log in as analyst@exargen.com. Step 6: Navigate to /compliance and verify export buttons are visible.
Expected Result: Export buttons are only shown to users with the compliance.export capability.

Title: Manage button requires compliance.manage capability
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /compliance. Step 3: Look for the Manage button. Step 4: Verify the Manage button is hidden or disabled for analyst. Step 5: Log out and log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 6: Navigate to /compliance and verify the Manage button is visible and clickable.
Expected Result: Manage button is only available to users with compliance.manage capability.

Title: Score recalculation after changing control status
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance and note the GDPR framework score. Step 2: Expand GDPR and find a not-assessed control. Step 3: Assess the control as Compliant. Step 4: Verify the GDPR framework score increases. Step 5: Verify the overall compliance score also updates. Step 6: Note the new scores for comparison.
Expected Result: Framework and overall scores recalculate immediately when a control status changes.

Title: Partial compliance counts as 50 percent in score
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance and expand a framework where all controls are not-assessed. Step 2: Assess one control as Partial. Step 3: Calculate expected score: 1 partial out of N controls = 50/N percent. Step 4: Verify the framework score matches the expected calculation. Step 5: Verify partial controls contribute 50% weight.
Expected Result: Partial compliance status contributes 50% weight to the compliance score calculation.

Title: Not-applicable controls excluded from scoring
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance and expand a framework. Step 2: Assess a control as Not Applicable. Step 3: Verify the total control count for scoring decreases by one. Step 4: Verify the framework score is calculated excluding the N/A control. Step 5: Verify the N/A control shows a distinct badge or indicator.
Expected Result: Controls marked as Not Applicable are excluded from score calculation denominators.

Title: Empty framework with all not-assessed shows 0 percent
Preconditions: A framework where no controls have been assessed
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance. Step 2: Find a framework where all controls are in not-assessed status. Step 3: Verify the framework score displays as 0%. Step 4: Verify the compliant count is 0. Step 5: Verify the pending or not-assessed count equals the total control count.
Expected Result: A framework with all controls in not-assessed status shows 0% compliance score.

Title: Framework with all controls compliant shows 100 percent
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance and expand a small framework like GDPR. Step 2: Assess all 10 GDPR controls as Compliant. Step 3: Verify the GDPR framework score shows 100%. Step 4: Verify the compliant count equals 10. Step 5: Verify the non-compliant and partial counts are 0.
Expected Result: A framework with all controls marked compliant shows 100% compliance score.

Title: Assessment without evidence succeeds
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance and expand a framework. Step 2: Click a control status badge to open assessment dialog. Step 3: Select a status. Step 4: Enter findings text. Step 5: Do not add any evidence references. Step 6: Submit the assessment. Step 7: Verify the assessment saves successfully without evidence.
Expected Result: Assessments can be submitted without evidence references and are saved successfully.

Title: Assessment with multiple evidence references
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance and open assessment dialog for a control. Step 2: Select Compliant status. Step 3: Add evidence reference Penetration test report 2025. Step 4: Add evidence reference ISO 27001 certificate. Step 5: Add evidence reference Internal audit findings Q1. Step 6: Submit and verify all three evidence references are saved.
Expected Result: Multiple evidence references can be added to a single assessment.

Title: HIPAA category filter tabs display correctly
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at /compliance with HIPAA expanded
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance. Step 2: Expand the HIPAA framework. Step 3: Verify category filter tabs are displayed. Step 4: Verify tabs match HIPAA safeguard categories. Step 5: Click a category tab. Step 6: Verify only controls in that category are shown.
Expected Result: HIPAA framework shows category filter tabs that correctly filter controls by safeguard type.

Title: GDPR category filter tabs display correctly
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at /compliance with GDPR expanded
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance. Step 2: Expand the GDPR framework. Step 3: Verify category filter tabs are displayed. Step 4: Click each category tab. Step 5: Verify controls filter correctly per category. Step 6: Click All tab and verify all 10 controls display.
Expected Result: GDPR framework shows category filter tabs that correctly filter the displayed controls.

Title: Clicking All category tab shows all controls
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with a category filter active
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance and expand a framework. Step 2: Click a specific category tab to filter controls. Step 3: Verify a subset of controls is shown. Step 4: Click the All tab. Step 5: Verify all controls for the framework are displayed again. Step 6: Verify the count matches the framework total.
Expected Result: The All category tab removes the category filter and shows all controls for the framework.

Title: Export CSV contains correct column headers
Steps: Step 1: Export compliance report as CSV. Step 2: Open the CSV file in a text editor. Step 3: Verify the header row contains Framework Version ControlID Title Category Status. Step 4: Verify additional columns for Findings Evidence and AssessedBy exist. Step 5: Verify no extra or missing columns.
Expected Result: CSV export header row contains all expected columns for compliance reporting.

Title: Export CSV includes assessment findings
Preconditions: CSV export file downloaded after assessments
Steps: Step 1: Assess several controls with different statuses and findings. Step 2: Export compliance report as CSV. Step 3: Open the CSV file. Step 4: Verify controls that were assessed have findings text in the appropriate column. Step 5: Verify controls that were not assessed have empty findings.
Expected Result: CSV export includes the findings text for assessed controls.

Title: Auditor can view compliance but not assess or manage
Steps: Step 1: Log in as auditor@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /compliance. Step 3: Verify the page loads and frameworks are visible. Step 4: Verify clicking a control badge does not open assessment dialog. Step 5: Verify the Manage button is hidden. Step 6: Verify the Export button is visible (compliance.export).
Expected Result: Auditor can view and export compliance data but cannot assess controls or manage frameworks.

Title: Org Admin can access compliance management
Steps: Step 1: Log in as orgadmin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /compliance. Step 3: Verify the compliance page loads. Step 4: Verify the Manage button is visible and clickable. Step 5: Click Manage and verify framework management view opens. Step 6: Verify assessment dialog works for controls.
Expected Result: Organization Administrator has full access to compliance including assessment and management.

Title: Viewer cannot export compliance data
Steps: Step 1: Log in as viewer@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /compliance. Step 3: Verify the page loads with framework data visible. Step 4: Look for Export CSV and Export JSON buttons. Step 5: Verify the buttons are hidden or disabled. Step 6: Attempt to call the export API directly via dev tools and verify 403.
Expected Result: Viewer without compliance.export capability cannot export compliance reports.

Title: API enforces compliance.assess on assessment endpoint
Preconditions: Logged in as viewer@exargen.com with dev tools open
Steps: Step 1: Log in as viewer@exargen.com. Step 2: Open dev tools console. Step 3: Execute a POST fetch to the compliance assessment API endpoint with valid control data. Step 4: Verify the response is 403 Forbidden. Step 5: Verify no assessment is created.
Expected Result: API endpoint for compliance assessment returns 403 for users without compliance.assess capability.

Title: Framework card shows compliant count
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at /compliance after some assessments
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance. Step 2: Look at a framework card. Step 3: Verify the card displays the number of compliant controls. Step 4: Assess another control as Compliant. Step 5: Verify the compliant count on the card increments.
Expected Result: Framework cards show the count of compliant controls and update dynamically.

Title: Framework card shows non-compliant count
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance. Step 2: Assess a control as Non-Compliant. Step 3: Verify the framework card shows a non-compliant count. Step 4: Verify the count matches the number of non-compliant controls. Step 5: Verify the count is displayed with a red indicator.
Expected Result: Framework cards display the non-compliant control count with appropriate visual indicator.

Title: Framework card shows partial count
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance. Step 2: Assess a control as Partial. Step 3: Verify the framework card shows a partial count. Step 4: Verify the count matches the number of partial controls. Step 5: Verify the count is displayed with a yellow indicator.
Expected Result: Framework cards display the partial compliance control count with appropriate visual indicator.

Title: Framework card shows pending/not-assessed count
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance. Step 2: Look at a framework card with unassessed controls. Step 3: Verify the card displays the pending or not-assessed count. Step 4: Verify the count matches controls that have not been assessed. Step 5: Verify it is shown with a gray indicator.
Expected Result: Framework cards display the not-assessed control count with gray visual indicator.

Title: Export filtered framework produces subset data
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at /compliance with GDPR filter active
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance. Step 2: Set the framework filter to GDPR only. Step 3: Click Export CSV. Step 4: Open the exported file. Step 5: Verify only GDPR controls are included (10 rows). Step 6: Verify no controls from other frameworks are present.
Expected Result: Export respects the active framework filter and only includes filtered data.

Title: Control shows correct category label
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with controls expanded
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance and expand a framework. Step 2: View the control list. Step 3: Verify each control displays its category label. Step 4: Verify the category matches when clicking the corresponding category tab. Step 5: Verify categories are consistent across the framework.
Expected Result: Each control displays its correct category label matching the framework category taxonomy.

Title: Assessment dialog shows control ID and title
Preconditions: Assessment dialog opened for a specific control
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /compliance and expand a framework. Step 2: Note the ID and title of a control. Step 3: Click the status badge to open assessment dialog. Step 4: Verify the dialog header shows the same control ID. Step 5: Verify the control title is displayed in the dialog.
Expected Result: Assessment dialog header displays the correct control ID and title for the selected control.

Title: Compliance page is accessible via sidebar navigation
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Look at the sidebar navigation. Step 3: Verify a Compliance link is present in the sidebar. Step 4: Click the Compliance link. Step 5: Verify navigation to /compliance page.
Expected Result: Compliance module is accessible through the sidebar navigation menu.

Title: Compliance page shows loading state
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with slow network (throttled)
Steps: Step 1: Open dev tools and throttle network to Slow 3G. Step 2: Navigate to /compliance. Step 3: Verify a loading spinner or skeleton is displayed while data loads. Step 4: Wait for the data to load. Step 5: Verify the loading state is replaced by actual framework data.
Expected Result: Compliance page shows a loading indicator while fetching framework and control data.

Title: Compliance page handles API error gracefully
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with compliance API returning error
Steps: Step 1: Open dev tools and block the compliance API endpoint using Network request blocking. Step 2: Navigate to /compliance. Step 3: Verify an error message is displayed instead of a blank page. Step 4: Verify the error message is user-friendly. Step 5: Remove the block and refresh to verify normal loading.
Expected Result: Compliance page displays a user-friendly error message when the API fails to respond.

Title: Navigate to security settings and find MFA section
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/security. Step 3: Verify the security settings page loads. Step 4: Locate the Multi-Factor Authentication section. Step 5: Verify an Enable MFA button is visible.
Expected Result: Security settings page displays the MFA section with an Enable MFA button.

Title: Enable MFA shows QR code
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/security. Step 2: Click the Enable MFA button. Step 3: Verify a QR code is displayed. Step 4: Verify the QR code contains an otpauth:// URI. Step 5: Verify a manual entry key is also shown as an alternative to scanning.
Expected Result: Clicking Enable MFA generates and displays a QR code with otpauth:// URI and a manual entry key.

Title: Confirm MFA setup with valid TOTP code
Preconditions: MFA QR code displayed; authenticator app configured
Steps: Step 1: Scan the QR code with an authenticator app. Step 2: Generate a TOTP code from the app. Step 3: Enter the 6-digit code in the confirmation field. Step 4: Click Verify or Confirm. Step 5: Verify MFA is now enabled with a success message. Step 6: Verify backup codes are generated and displayed.
Expected Result: Valid TOTP code confirms MFA setup successfully and triggers backup code generation.

Title: Confirm MFA with invalid code shows error
Preconditions: MFA QR code displayed; authenticator app configured
Steps: Step 1: Scan the QR code with an authenticator app. Step 2: Enter an incorrect 6-digit code like 000000. Step 3: Click Verify or Confirm. Step 4: Verify an error message is displayed. Step 5: Verify MFA remains disabled. Step 6: Verify the QR code remains available for retry.
Expected Result: Invalid TOTP code is rejected with an error message and MFA remains disabled.

Title: MFA enabled status indicator on security page
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/security. Step 2: Verify the MFA section shows an enabled status indicator. Step 3: Verify the Enable MFA button is replaced with a Disable MFA option. Step 4: Verify a green badge or checkmark indicates MFA is active.
Expected Result: Security page shows clear MFA enabled status indicator after successful setup.

Title: MFA login flow shows TOTP input after password
Steps: Step 1: Log out and navigate to /login. Step 2: Enter email admin@exargen.com. Step 3: Enter password Admin123!. Step 4: Click Sign In. Step 5: Verify a second step appears requesting a TOTP code. Step 6: Verify the TOTP input field accepts 6 digits.
Expected Result: After successful password authentication the login flow presents a TOTP verification step.

Title: MFA login with valid TOTP proceeds to dashboard
Steps: Step 1: Enter email and password at /login. Step 2: At the TOTP prompt generate a code from the authenticator app. Step 3: Enter the valid 6-digit TOTP code. Step 4: Click Verify. Step 5: Verify redirect to /dashboard. Step 6: Verify the user is fully authenticated.
Expected Result: Valid TOTP code completes MFA login and redirects to the dashboard.

Title: MFA login with invalid TOTP shows error
Steps: Step 1: Enter email and password at /login. Step 2: At the TOTP prompt enter an invalid code 123456. Step 3: Click Verify. Step 4: Verify an error message about invalid code is shown. Step 5: Verify the user remains on the TOTP verification page. Step 6: Verify the user is not authenticated.
Expected Result: Invalid TOTP code is rejected with an error and the user remains unauthenticated.

Title: MFA login with backup code succeeds
Steps: Step 1: Log out and navigate to /login. Step 2: Enter email and password for the MFA-enabled account. Step 3: At the TOTP prompt click Use backup code. Step 4: Enter one of the 10 backup codes. Step 5: Click Verify. Step 6: Verify login succeeds and redirect to /dashboard.
Expected Result: Backup code can be used as an alternative to TOTP for MFA verification.

Title: Backup codes are single-use
Preconditions: MFA enabled; one backup code was used in previous test
Steps: Step 1: Log out and navigate to /login. Step 2: Enter email and password. Step 3: At the TOTP prompt select use backup code. Step 4: Enter the same backup code used in the previous test. Step 5: Click Verify. Step 6: Verify the code is rejected with an error. Step 7: Verify the user cannot authenticate with a used backup code.
Expected Result: Backup codes are single-use and cannot be reused after first successful authentication.

Title: Ten backup codes generated on MFA setup
Steps: Step 1: Complete MFA setup with valid TOTP code. Step 2: Verify backup codes are displayed. Step 3: Count the number of backup codes shown. Step 4: Verify exactly 10 backup codes are generated. Step 5: Verify each code is a unique alphanumeric string.
Expected Result: MFA setup generates exactly 10 unique backup codes for account recovery.

Title: MFA disable requires current TOTP code
Preconditions: MFA enabled for the account at /settings/security
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/security. Step 2: Click Disable MFA. Step 3: Verify a prompt asks for the current TOTP code. Step 4: Enter a valid TOTP code from the authenticator app. Step 5: Confirm the disable action. Step 6: Verify MFA is disabled and the Enable MFA button reappears.
Expected Result: Disabling MFA requires verification with a current TOTP code for security.

Title: MFA disable with wrong code fails
Preconditions: MFA enabled for the account at /settings/security
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/security. Step 2: Click Disable MFA. Step 3: Enter an incorrect TOTP code 000000. Step 4: Confirm the disable action. Step 5: Verify an error message is shown. Step 6: Verify MFA remains enabled.
Expected Result: Attempting to disable MFA with an incorrect TOTP code fails and MFA stays enabled.

Title: Navigate to identity settings page
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/identity. Step 3: Verify the identity settings page loads. Step 4: Verify SSO provider management section is visible. Step 5: Verify SCIM token management section is visible.
Expected Result: Identity settings page loads with SSO provider and SCIM token management sections.

Title: Add Google SSO provider
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/identity. Step 2: Click Add Provider or Add SSO Provider. Step 3: Select Google as the provider type. Step 4: Enter client ID and client secret. Step 5: Enter the allowed domain such as exargen.com. Step 6: Click Save. Step 7: Verify the Google provider appears in the provider list.
Expected Result: Google SSO provider can be configured with client credentials and domain restriction.

Title: Add Azure AD SSO provider
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/identity. Step 2: Click Add Provider. Step 3: Select Azure AD as the provider type. Step 4: Enter client ID client secret and tenant ID. Step 5: Enter the allowed domain. Step 6: Click Save. Step 7: Verify the Azure AD provider appears in the provider list.
Expected Result: Azure AD SSO provider can be configured with Azure-specific credentials.

Title: Add Okta SSO provider
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/identity. Step 2: Click Add Provider. Step 3: Select Okta as the provider type. Step 4: Enter client ID client secret and Okta domain. Step 5: Enter the allowed domain. Step 6: Click Save. Step 7: Verify the Okta provider appears in the provider list.
Expected Result: Okta SSO provider can be configured with Okta-specific credentials and domain.

Title: Edit SSO provider details
Preconditions: At least one SSO provider configured
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/identity. Step 2: Click on an existing SSO provider. Step 3: Modify the allowed domain. Step 4: Update the client secret. Step 5: Click Save. Step 6: Verify the updated details are persisted.
Expected Result: SSO provider configuration can be edited and changes are saved.

Title: Delete SSO provider
Preconditions: At least one SSO provider configured
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/identity. Step 2: Find the SSO provider to delete. Step 3: Click the Delete button. Step 4: Confirm the deletion. Step 5: Verify the provider is removed from the list. Step 6: Verify a success notification is shown.
Expected Result: SSO provider can be deleted from the identity settings.

Title: SSO provider appears on login page
Preconditions: At least one SSO provider configured and active
Steps: Step 1: Log out. Step 2: Navigate to /login. Step 3: Verify SSO provider buttons appear below the password login form. Step 4: Verify the button shows the provider name (e.g. Sign in with Google). Step 5: Verify the provider icon is displayed.
Expected Result: Configured SSO providers appear as login options on the login page.

Title: SSO authorization redirect to provider URL
Preconditions: SSO provider configured; on login page
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /login. Step 2: Click the SSO provider button (e.g. Sign in with Google). Step 3: Verify the browser redirects to the provider authorization URL. Step 4: Verify the URL contains client_id redirect_uri and scope parameters. Step 5: Verify the state parameter is included for CSRF protection.
Expected Result: Clicking SSO button initiates OAuth flow with redirect to the provider authorization endpoint.

Title: SSO callback handles success state
Steps: Step 1: Complete the SSO authorization at the provider. Step 2: Verify the callback redirects back to the application. Step 3: Verify the application processes the authorization code. Step 4: Verify the user is authenticated and redirected to /dashboard. Step 5: Verify the session is created.
Expected Result: SSO callback successfully processes the authorization code and creates an authenticated session.

Title: SSO callback handles error state
Preconditions: SSO authorization denied or failed at provider
Steps: Step 1: Initiate SSO login. Step 2: At the provider deny the authorization or simulate an error. Step 3: Verify the callback redirects back to the login page. Step 4: Verify an error message is displayed. Step 5: Verify no session is created.
Expected Result: SSO callback gracefully handles authorization errors and displays an appropriate message.

Title: SSO JIT user provisioning creates new user
Preconditions: SSO provider configured; new user not in BYOC system
Steps: Step 1: Configure an SSO provider. Step 2: Authenticate with a user email that does not exist in BYOC. Step 3: Complete the SSO flow. Step 4: Verify the user is automatically created in the system. Step 5: Verify the user is assigned a default role. Step 6: Verify the user can access the dashboard.
Expected Result: Just-In-Time provisioning automatically creates a new user account when authenticating via SSO for the first time.

Title: SSO account linking by email for existing user
Preconditions: SSO provider configured; user exists with matching email
Steps: Step 1: Ensure a user exists with email analyst@exargen.com. Step 2: Configure SSO provider for exargen.com domain. Step 3: Authenticate via SSO with analyst@exargen.com. Step 4: Verify the SSO identity is linked to the existing user account. Step 5: Verify the user retains their existing roles and permissions.
Expected Result: SSO authentication links to an existing account when the email matches preserving roles and permissions.

Title: Create SCIM token on identity settings page
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/identity. Step 2: Locate the SCIM configuration section. Step 3: Click Create SCIM Token. Step 4: Enter a description such as Okta SCIM Integration. Step 5: Click Create. Step 6: Verify the token value is displayed. Step 7: Copy and save the token.
Expected Result: SCIM token is created and the token value is displayed for copying.

Title: SCIM token shown only once after creation
Steps: Step 1: Note the SCIM token value displayed after creation. Step 2: Close the dialog or navigate away. Step 3: Return to /settings/identity. Step 4: Find the SCIM token in the list. Step 5: Verify the full token value is not visible. Step 6: Verify only a masked or partial token is shown.
Expected Result: SCIM token secret is displayed only once at creation time and cannot be retrieved afterward.

Title: List SCIM tokens
Preconditions: At least one SCIM token created
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/identity. Step 2: Locate the SCIM tokens list. Step 3: Verify the created token is listed. Step 4: Verify the description is shown. Step 5: Verify the creation date is displayed. Step 6: Verify a Revoke button is available.
Expected Result: SCIM tokens list shows all tokens with descriptions creation dates and revoke options.

Title: Revoke SCIM token
Preconditions: At least one SCIM token exists
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/identity. Step 2: Find the SCIM token to revoke. Step 3: Click the Revoke button. Step 4: Confirm the revocation. Step 5: Verify the token is removed from the list or marked as revoked. Step 6: Verify a success notification is shown.
Expected Result: SCIM token can be revoked removing its ability to authenticate SCIM requests.

Title: GET /api/scim/v2/Users returns user list
Steps: Step 1: Open a REST client or dev tools console. Step 2: Execute GET request to /api/scim/v2/Users with Bearer token header. Step 3: Verify the response is 200 OK. Step 4: Verify the response follows SCIM ListResponse schema. Step 5: Verify Resources array contains user objects. Step 6: Verify totalResults matches the number of users.
Expected Result: SCIM Users endpoint returns a properly formatted ListResponse with user resources.

Title: POST /api/scim/v2/Users creates a new user
Preconditions: SCIM token available; valid user payload prepared
Steps: Step 1: Prepare a SCIM user creation payload with userName displayName and emails. Step 2: Execute POST to /api/scim/v2/Users with Bearer token and JSON body. Step 3: Verify the response is 201 Created. Step 4: Verify the response contains the created user with an assigned id. Step 5: Verify the user appears in the BYOC user list.
Expected Result: SCIM POST Users endpoint creates a new user and returns the created resource with 201 status.

Title: GET /api/scim/v2/Users/:id returns single user
Preconditions: A user exists in the system with known SCIM id
Steps: Step 1: Execute GET to /api/scim/v2/Users/{id} with Bearer token. Step 2: Verify the response is 200 OK. Step 3: Verify the response contains the user resource. Step 4: Verify userName displayName and emails are present. Step 5: Verify the id matches the requested id.
Expected Result: SCIM get user by ID returns the correct user resource with all attributes.

Title: PATCH /api/scim/v2/Users/:id updates user
Preconditions: A user exists with known SCIM id
Steps: Step 1: Prepare a SCIM PATCH payload to update displayName. Step 2: Execute PATCH to /api/scim/v2/Users/{id} with Bearer token and JSON body. Step 3: Verify the response is 200 OK. Step 4: Verify the response contains the updated user. Step 5: Verify the displayName has changed. Step 6: Execute GET to verify persistence.
Expected Result: SCIM PATCH Users endpoint updates user attributes and returns the updated resource.

Title: DELETE /api/scim/v2/Users/:id deactivates user
Preconditions: A user exists with known SCIM id
Steps: Step 1: Execute DELETE to /api/scim/v2/Users/{id} with Bearer token. Step 2: Verify the response is 204 No Content. Step 3: Execute GET to /api/scim/v2/Users/{id}. Step 4: Verify the user is deactivated (active: false). Step 5: Verify the deactivated user cannot log in to BYOC.
Expected Result: SCIM DELETE Users endpoint deactivates the user rather than hard-deleting.

Title: SCIM user filter by userName
Preconditions: SCIM token available; multiple users exist
Steps: Step 1: Execute GET to /api/scim/v2/Users?filter=userName eq analyst@exargen.com with Bearer token. Step 2: Verify the response is 200 OK. Step 3: Verify the Resources array contains exactly one user. Step 4: Verify the returned user has userName analyst@exargen.com. Step 5: Verify totalResults is 1.
Expected Result: SCIM user filter by userName returns only the matching user.

Title: SCIM pagination with startIndex and count
Preconditions: SCIM token available; multiple users exist
Steps: Step 1: Execute GET to /api/scim/v2/Users?startIndex=1&count=2 with Bearer token. Step 2: Verify the response is 200 OK. Step 3: Verify Resources contains at most 2 users. Step 4: Verify startIndex in response matches requested value. Step 5: Verify itemsPerPage matches the count parameter. Step 6: Execute another request with startIndex=3 to get the next page.
Expected Result: SCIM pagination returns the correct subset of users based on startIndex and count parameters.

Title: GET /api/scim/v2/Groups lists roles
Steps: Step 1: Execute GET to /api/scim/v2/Groups with Bearer token. Step 2: Verify the response is 200 OK. Step 3: Verify the response follows SCIM ListResponse schema. Step 4: Verify Resources array contains group objects representing BYOC roles. Step 5: Verify each group has id displayName and members array.
Expected Result: SCIM Groups endpoint returns roles as groups with proper SCIM schema.

Title: GET /api/scim/v2/Groups/:id returns role with members
Preconditions: A role/group exists with known id
Steps: Step 1: Execute GET to /api/scim/v2/Groups/{id} with Bearer token. Step 2: Verify the response is 200 OK. Step 3: Verify the group displayName matches the role name. Step 4: Verify the members array lists users assigned to the role. Step 5: Verify each member has value and display attributes.
Expected Result: SCIM get group by ID returns the role with its member list.

Title: PATCH /api/scim/v2/Groups/:id manages members
Preconditions: A role/group exists with known id
Steps: Step 1: Prepare a SCIM PATCH payload to add a user to the group members. Step 2: Execute PATCH to /api/scim/v2/Groups/{id} with Bearer token. Step 3: Verify the response is 200 OK. Step 4: Verify the members array includes the added user. Step 5: Execute GET to verify the membership persists.
Expected Result: SCIM PATCH Groups endpoint can add and remove members from role groups.

Title: GET /api/scim/v2/ServiceProviderConfig returns config
Steps: Step 1: Execute GET to /api/scim/v2/ServiceProviderConfig with Bearer token. Step 2: Verify the response is 200 OK. Step 3: Verify the response contains patch supported flag. Step 4: Verify bulk and filter configuration are present. Step 5: Verify the schemas attribute references urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:ServiceProviderConfig.
Expected Result: SCIM ServiceProviderConfig endpoint returns supported features and capabilities.

Title: GET /api/scim/v2/Schemas returns schemas
Steps: Step 1: Execute GET to /api/scim/v2/Schemas with Bearer token. Step 2: Verify the response is 200 OK. Step 3: Verify User and Group schemas are included. Step 4: Verify each schema lists its attributes with type and mutability. Step 5: Verify the schema URNs follow SCIM 2.0 conventions.
Expected Result: SCIM Schemas endpoint returns User and Group schema definitions.

Title: SCIM request without Bearer token returns 401
Steps: Step 1: Execute GET to /api/scim/v2/Users without any Authorization header. Step 2: Verify the response is 401 Unauthorized. Step 3: Execute GET to /api/scim/v2/Groups without Authorization. Step 4: Verify 401. Step 5: Execute GET to /api/scim/v2/ServiceProviderConfig without Authorization. Step 6: Verify 401.
Expected Result: All SCIM endpoints require Bearer token authentication and return 401 without it.

Title: SCIM request with revoked token returns 401
Preconditions: A SCIM token has been revoked
Steps: Step 1: Take the revoked SCIM token value. Step 2: Execute GET to /api/scim/v2/Users with the revoked token as Bearer. Step 3: Verify the response is 401 Unauthorized. Step 4: Execute POST to /api/scim/v2/Users with the revoked token. Step 5: Verify 401. Step 6: Verify no data is returned.
Expected Result: Revoked SCIM tokens are immediately rejected with 401 on all SCIM endpoints.

Title: Identity settings page gated by admin.sso.manage
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate directly to /settings/identity. Step 3: Verify access is denied or the page redirects. Step 4: Verify no SSO or SCIM configuration is visible. Step 5: Verify the sidebar does not show an Identity settings link for analyst.
Expected Result: Identity settings page is restricted to users with admin.sso.manage capability.

Title: Non-admin cannot access /settings/identity
Steps: Step 1: Log in as viewer@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate directly to /settings/identity. Step 3: Verify access is denied. Step 4: Log in as auditor@exargen.com. Step 5: Navigate to /settings/identity. Step 6: Verify access is denied for auditor as well.
Expected Result: Only users with admin.sso.manage can access the identity settings page.

Title: Admin can access identity settings
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/identity. Step 3: Verify the page loads successfully. Step 4: Verify SSO provider management section is visible. Step 5: Verify SCIM token management section is visible. Step 6: Verify all management buttons are functional.
Expected Result: Platform Administrator with admin.sso.manage can fully access identity settings.

Title: Org Admin can access identity settings
Steps: Step 1: Log in as orgadmin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/identity. Step 3: Verify the page loads successfully. Step 4: Verify SSO provider management is accessible. Step 5: Verify SCIM token management is accessible.
Expected Result: Organization Administrator can access and manage identity settings.

Title: MFA secrets encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM
Preconditions: Admin access to verify encryption; MFA enabled for a user
Steps: Step 1: Enable MFA for a user account. Step 2: Access the database or API to inspect the stored MFA secret. Step 3: Verify the MFA secret is not stored in plaintext. Step 4: Verify the stored value is encrypted (Base64 or hex encoded ciphertext). Step 5: Verify the encryption uses AES-256-GCM as indicated by the ciphertext format.
Expected Result: MFA secrets are encrypted at rest using AES-256-GCM and are not stored in plaintext.

Title: SSO client secrets encrypted at rest
Preconditions: Admin access to verify encryption; SSO provider configured
Steps: Step 1: Configure an SSO provider with a client secret. Step 2: Access the database or API to inspect the stored client secret. Step 3: Verify the client secret is not stored in plaintext. Step 4: Verify the stored value is encrypted. Step 5: Verify the application can still use the secret for SSO flows after encryption.
Expected Result: SSO client secrets are encrypted at rest and not stored in plaintext in the database.

Title: MFA pending token expires after 5 minutes
Preconditions: MFA enabled; login initiated but TOTP not entered
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /login and enter valid email and password. Step 2: At the TOTP prompt do not enter a code. Step 3: Wait for 5 minutes. Step 4: Enter a valid TOTP code. Step 5: Click Verify. Step 6: Verify the verification fails with a session expired message. Step 7: Verify the user must restart the login flow.
Expected Result: MFA pending token expires after 5 minutes requiring the user to restart authentication.

Title: SSO PKCE challenge generation uses S256 method
Preconditions: SSO provider configured; dev tools open
Steps: Step 1: Open dev tools Network tab. Step 2: Click the SSO login button. Step 3: Inspect the authorization redirect URL. Step 4: Verify code_challenge parameter is present. Step 5: Verify code_challenge_method is set to S256. Step 6: Verify a code_verifier is stored for the callback exchange.
Expected Result: SSO flow uses PKCE with S256 method for secure authorization code exchange.

Title: MFA setup is audit logged
Preconditions: MFA was just enabled; logged in as admin@exargen.com
Steps: Step 1: Enable MFA for the account. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/audit-log or /audit. Step 3: Search for MFA-related audit entries. Step 4: Verify an entry exists for MFA setup or mfa.enable action. Step 5: Verify the actor matches the user who enabled MFA. Step 6: Verify the timestamp is correct.
Expected Result: MFA setup events are recorded in the audit log with actor and timestamp.

Title: MFA disable is audit logged
Preconditions: MFA was just disabled; logged in as admin@exargen.com
Steps: Step 1: Disable MFA for the account. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/audit-log or /audit. Step 3: Search for MFA disable entries. Step 4: Verify an entry exists for mfa.disable action. Step 5: Verify the actor and timestamp are correct.
Expected Result: MFA disable events are recorded in the audit log.

Title: SSO provider create is audit logged
Preconditions: SSO provider was just created; logged in as admin@exargen.com
Steps: Step 1: Create an SSO provider. Step 2: Navigate to /settings/audit-log or /audit. Step 3: Search for SSO-related audit entries. Step 4: Verify an entry exists for SSO provider creation. Step 5: Verify the provider type and actor are recorded. Step 6: Verify no client secret is logged.
Expected Result: SSO provider creation events are recorded in the audit log without exposing client secrets.

Title: MFA QR code contains correct issuer and account
Steps: Step 1: Click Enable MFA on the security page. Step 2: Inspect the QR code URI (or the manual entry details). Step 3: Verify the issuer is set to BYOC or the application name. Step 4: Verify the account label includes the user email. Step 5: Verify the algorithm is SHA1 and digits is 6.
Expected Result: MFA QR code otpauth URI contains the correct issuer application name and user email.

Title: MFA login page shows correct branding
Preconditions: MFA enabled; at TOTP verification step
Steps: Step 1: Initiate login for an MFA-enabled account. Step 2: At the TOTP verification step verify the BYOC branding is present. Step 3: Verify the page title or heading indicates two-factor authentication. Step 4: Verify a Use backup code link is available. Step 5: Verify the input field is clearly labeled.
Expected Result: MFA TOTP verification page displays proper branding and clear instructions.

Title: Backup codes display format is user-friendly
Preconditions: Backup codes displayed after MFA setup
Steps: Step 1: Complete MFA setup to trigger backup code display. Step 2: Verify backup codes are shown in a readable format. Step 3: Verify a copy or download button is available. Step 4: Verify a warning message advises saving codes securely. Step 5: Verify codes are displayed in a monospace font for clarity.
Expected Result: Backup codes are displayed in a user-friendly format with copy/download options and security warnings.

Title: SSO provider list shows all configured providers
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/identity. Step 2: Verify all configured SSO providers are listed. Step 3: Verify each provider shows its type (Google Azure Okta). Step 4: Verify each shows its allowed domain. Step 5: Verify Edit and Delete buttons are available for each.
Expected Result: SSO provider list accurately displays all configured providers with their details.

Title: SSO provider requires client ID and secret
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/identity. Step 2: Click Add Provider. Step 3: Select a provider type. Step 4: Leave client ID empty and try to save. Step 5: Verify a validation error for client ID. Step 6: Enter client ID but leave secret empty. Step 7: Verify a validation error for client secret.
Expected Result: SSO provider creation validates that client ID and client secret are required fields.

Title: SCIM user creation with minimal payload
Steps: Step 1: Prepare a minimal SCIM user payload with only userName and emails. Step 2: Execute POST to /api/scim/v2/Users with Bearer token. Step 3: Verify the response is 201 Created. Step 4: Verify the user is created with default values for optional fields. Step 5: Verify the response includes the generated id.
Expected Result: SCIM user creation succeeds with minimal required fields.

Title: SCIM user creation with duplicate userName fails
Preconditions: SCIM token available; user with email already exists
Steps: Step 1: Execute POST to /api/scim/v2/Users with a userName that already exists. Step 2: Verify the response is 409 Conflict. Step 3: Verify the error message indicates duplicate userName. Step 4: Verify no duplicate user is created.
Expected Result: SCIM user creation rejects duplicate userNames with 409 Conflict.

Title: SCIM group list includes all built-in roles
Steps: Step 1: Execute GET to /api/scim/v2/Groups with Bearer token. Step 2: Verify the response contains groups for all 7 built-in roles. Step 3: Verify Platform Administrator and Viewer groups are present. Step 4: Verify totalResults is at least 7. Step 5: Verify each group has the correct displayName.
Expected Result: SCIM Groups endpoint lists all 7 built-in BYOC roles as groups.

Title: ServiceProviderConfig indicates supported operations
Steps: Step 1: Execute GET to /api/scim/v2/ServiceProviderConfig. Step 2: Verify patch.supported is true. Step 3: Verify filter.supported indicates filtering capability. Step 4: Verify changePassword.supported status. Step 5: Verify the response includes documentation URI if available.
Expected Result: ServiceProviderConfig accurately reports which SCIM operations are supported.

Title: SCIM request with invalid token format returns 401
Steps: Step 1: Execute GET to /api/scim/v2/Users with Authorization header set to Bearer invalid-token-format. Step 2: Verify the response is 401 Unauthorized. Step 3: Execute with Authorization header set to Basic credentials. Step 4: Verify 401. Step 5: Verify no user data is returned.
Expected Result: SCIM endpoints reject invalid or malformed tokens with 401.

Title: MFA TOTP code input accepts only 6 digits
Preconditions: MFA enabled; at TOTP verification step
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /login and enter valid credentials. Step 2: At TOTP prompt enter 5 digits. Step 3: Verify the submit button is disabled or validation prevents submission. Step 4: Enter 7 digits. Step 5: Verify the input truncates or rejects extra digits. Step 6: Enter exactly 6 digits and verify submission is allowed.
Expected Result: TOTP input field validates that exactly 6 digits are entered.

Title: After MFA disable login no longer requires TOTP
Preconditions: MFA was disabled for the account
Steps: Step 1: Disable MFA for admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Log out. Step 3: Navigate to /login. Step 4: Enter admin@exargen.com and Admin123!. Step 5: Click Sign In. Step 6: Verify login proceeds directly to /dashboard without TOTP step.
Expected Result: After disabling MFA the login flow no longer requires TOTP verification.

Title: SSO login creates session with correct role
Preconditions: SSO provider configured; user with analyst role authenticates via SSO
Steps: Step 1: Authenticate via SSO with an account linked to analyst@exargen.com. Step 2: Verify redirect to /dashboard after successful SSO. Step 3: Verify the session shows the Security Analyst role. Step 4: Verify the user has analyst capabilities. Step 5: Navigate to /scans and verify access is granted.
Expected Result: SSO login creates a session with the correct role and capabilities for the authenticated user.

Title: SCIM GET Users returns correct schema attributes
Steps: Step 1: Execute GET to /api/scim/v2/Users with Bearer token. Step 2: Verify each user resource has schemas array. Step 3: Verify schemas includes urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:User. Step 4: Verify userName id and meta attributes are present. Step 5: Verify meta includes resourceType and created dates.
Expected Result: SCIM user resources include proper schema URNs and required attributes.

Title: SCIM GET Groups returns correct schema attributes
Steps: Step 1: Execute GET to /api/scim/v2/Groups with Bearer token. Step 2: Verify each group resource has schemas array. Step 3: Verify schemas includes urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:Group. Step 4: Verify displayName id and members attributes are present. Step 5: Verify meta includes resourceType.
Expected Result: SCIM group resources include proper schema URNs and required attributes.

Title: SSO provider domain restriction enforced
Preconditions: SSO provider configured with domain restriction exargen.com
Steps: Step 1: Configure SSO provider with allowed domain exargen.com. Step 2: Attempt SSO login with an email from a different domain (e.g. user@other.com). Step 3: Verify the login is rejected. Step 4: Verify an error message about domain mismatch is shown. Step 5: Attempt with exargen.com email and verify success.
Expected Result: SSO domain restriction blocks authentication from unauthorized email domains.

Title: SCIM PATCH can deactivate user via active attribute
Preconditions: SCIM token available; user exists with active true
Steps: Step 1: Prepare a SCIM PATCH payload setting active to false. Step 2: Execute PATCH to /api/scim/v2/Users/{id} with Bearer token. Step 3: Verify the response is 200 OK. Step 4: Verify the user active attribute is now false. Step 5: Verify the deactivated user cannot log in.
Expected Result: SCIM PATCH can deactivate a user by setting the active attribute to false.

Title: SCIM filter with unsupported operator returns error
Steps: Step 1: Execute GET to /api/scim/v2/Users?filter=userName gt test with Bearer token. Step 2: Verify the response indicates the operator is not supported. Step 3: Verify the error follows SCIM error schema. Step 4: Verify status is 400 or appropriate error code.
Expected Result: SCIM filter endpoint returns appropriate error for unsupported filter operators.

Title: MFA setup can be retried after failure
Preconditions: MFA setup failed with invalid code
Steps: Step 1: Start MFA setup at /settings/security. Step 2: Enter an invalid TOTP code. Step 3: Verify error is shown and MFA remains disabled. Step 4: Click Enable MFA again. Step 5: Enter a valid TOTP code. Step 6: Verify MFA is now enabled successfully.
Expected Result: MFA setup can be retried after a failed attempt without issues.

Title: Multiple SCIM tokens can coexist
Preconditions: At least one SCIM token exists
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/identity. Step 2: Create a second SCIM token with description Azure AD SCIM. Step 3: Verify both tokens are listed. Step 4: Test the first token with GET /api/scim/v2/Users. Step 5: Test the second token with GET /api/scim/v2/Users. Step 6: Verify both tokens work independently.
Expected Result: Multiple SCIM tokens can be active simultaneously and authenticate independently.

Title: SCIM token creation is audit logged
Steps: Step 1: Create a SCIM token. Step 2: Navigate to the audit log. Step 3: Search for SCIM token creation entries. Step 4: Verify an entry exists for the token creation. Step 5: Verify the actor and timestamp are recorded.
Expected Result: SCIM token creation events are captured in the audit log.

Title: SCIM token revocation is audit logged
Steps: Step 1: Revoke a SCIM token. Step 2: Navigate to the audit log. Step 3: Search for SCIM token revocation entries. Step 4: Verify an entry exists for the token revocation. Step 5: Verify the actor and timestamp are recorded.
Expected Result: SCIM token revocation events are captured in the audit log.

Title: SSO provider edit is audit logged
Steps: Step 1: Edit an SSO provider configuration. Step 2: Navigate to the audit log. Step 3: Search for SSO provider edit entries. Step 4: Verify an entry exists for the configuration change. Step 5: Verify no client secret values are in the audit entry.
Expected Result: SSO provider configuration edits are audit logged without exposing secrets.

Title: SSO provider delete is audit logged
Steps: Step 1: Delete an SSO provider. Step 2: Navigate to the audit log. Step 3: Search for SSO provider deletion entries. Step 4: Verify an entry exists for the deletion. Step 5: Verify the provider type and actor are recorded.
Expected Result: SSO provider deletion events are captured in the audit log.

Title: SCIM user update via PUT replaces resource
Steps: Step 1: Prepare a full SCIM user resource payload with updated fields. Step 2: Execute PUT to /api/scim/v2/Users/{id} with Bearer token and full payload. Step 3: Verify the response is 200 OK. Step 4: Verify the user resource is fully replaced with the new data. Step 5: Verify fields not included in the PUT payload are cleared or defaulted.
Expected Result: SCIM PUT Users endpoint performs a full resource replacement.

Title: Identity settings page shows SSO and SCIM sections together
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /settings/identity. Step 2: Verify the page has a clear SSO Providers section. Step 3: Verify the page has a clear SCIM Configuration section. Step 4: Verify both sections are visible without scrolling or tab switching. Step 5: Verify the page layout is organized and user-friendly.
Expected Result: Identity settings page presents SSO and SCIM management in a unified organized layout.

Title: Create vulnerability scan with single target
