Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at https://byoc-rosy.vercel.app
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com with password Admin123!. Step 2: Navigate to /scans. Step 3: Click the New Scan button. Step 4: Enter scan name as Vuln Scan Test 1. Step 5: Select scan type Vulnerability Scan. Step 6: Enter a single target hostname such as example.com. Step 7: Click Create Scan. Step 8: Verify success message is displayed and scan appears in the list.
Expected Result: Vulnerability scan is created successfully with status queued. Scan appears in the scan list with the correct name type and target.

Title: Create port scan with multiple targets
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /scans. Step 2: Click New Scan. Step 3: Enter scan name as Multi-Target Port Scan. Step 4: Select scan type Port Scan. Step 5: Enter multiple targets separated by commas such as target1.example.com and target2.example.com. Step 6: Click Create Scan. Step 7: Verify the scan is created with all targets listed.
Expected Result: Port scan is created with multiple targets. All targets are stored and displayed in the scan detail.

Title: Create compliance scan
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /scans. Step 2: Click New Scan. Step 3: Enter scan name as Compliance Check. Step 4: Select scan type Compliance Scan. Step 5: Enter target hostname. Step 6: Click Create Scan. Step 7: Verify the scan is created with type badge showing Compliance Scan.
Expected Result: Compliance scan is created successfully with the correct type badge and queued status.

Title: Create full scan with all 8 modules
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /scans. Step 2: Click New Scan. Step 3: Enter scan name as Full Assessment Scan. Step 4: Select scan type Full Assessment. Step 5: Enter target hostname. Step 6: Click Create Scan. Step 7: Verify the scan is created with type badge showing Full Assessment. Step 8: Click into the scan detail and verify all 8 check modules are listed in the progress JSON.
Expected Result: Full assessment scan is created that will execute all 8 scanner check modules when run.

Title: Create scan with invalid or empty target shows error
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /scans. Step 2: Click New Scan. Step 3: Enter scan name as Empty Target Test. Step 4: Select scan type Vulnerability Scan. Step 5: Leave the target field empty. Step 6: Click Create Scan. Step 7: Verify a validation error message is displayed indicating target is required.
Expected Result: Form validation prevents scan creation with an empty target field and displays an appropriate error message.

Title: Create scan with duplicate name
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with at least one existing scan
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /scans. Step 2: Note the name of an existing scan. Step 3: Click New Scan. Step 4: Enter the same name as the existing scan. Step 5: Select any scan type and enter a target. Step 6: Click Create Scan. Step 7: Verify whether the system allows or rejects duplicate scan names.
Expected Result: System either prevents duplicate scan names with an error or allows them since scans are identified by unique ID.

Title: Scan auto-creates asset for unknown target
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /assets and note the current asset count. Step 2: Navigate to /scans. Step 3: Click New Scan. Step 4: Enter a target hostname that does not match any existing asset such as new-target-auto.example.com. Step 5: Create the scan. Step 6: Navigate to /assets. Step 7: Verify a new asset was automatically created for the target hostname. Step 8: Verify the asset count increased by one.
Expected Result: When a scan target does not match an existing asset a new asset record is automatically created for that hostname.

Title: New scan status is queued with progress JSON
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /scans. Step 2: Create a new scan with any valid configuration. Step 3: After creation click on the new scan to view its detail page. Step 4: Verify the status badge shows queued with yellow coloring. Step 5: Open browser dev tools and inspect the API response for the scan. Step 6: Verify the progress field contains a valid JSON object with completedChecks as an empty array.
Expected Result: Newly created scan has status queued and a progress JSON field with completedChecks as an empty array.

Title: RBAC scan.create required - viewer gets 403
Preconditions: Logged in as viewer@exargen.com with password Viewer123!
Steps: Step 1: Log in as viewer@exargen.com with password Viewer123!. Step 2: Open browser dev tools console. Step 3: Execute fetch against POST /api/scans with a valid scan creation payload. Step 4: Verify the response status is 403 Forbidden. Step 5: Navigate to /scans in the URL bar. Step 6: Verify no New Scan button is visible on the page.
Expected Result: Viewer role without scan.create capability receives 403 on scan creation API and the New Scan button is not rendered.

Title: Verify scan appears in scan list after creation
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /scans. Step 2: Note the current number of scans displayed. Step 3: Click New Scan. Step 4: Fill in scan name target and type. Step 5: Click Create Scan. Step 6: Verify the scan list now shows one additional scan. Step 7: Verify the new scan appears at the top of the list as the most recent.
Expected Result: After creating a scan the scan list refreshes and the new scan appears at the top sorted by most recent first.

Title: Scan list shows all scans with name type status targets and findings count
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at /scans with existing scans
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /scans. Step 3: Verify the scan list table loads. Step 4: Verify each scan row displays the scan name. Step 5: Verify each row shows the scan type. Step 6: Verify each row shows the status badge. Step 7: Verify targets are displayed. Step 8: Verify findings count is shown for completed scans.
Expected Result: Scan list page displays all scans in a table with columns for name type status targets and findings count.

Title: Scan type badges display correctly
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at /scans with scans of different types
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /scans. Step 2: Find a scan with type Vulnerability Scan and verify its badge text and styling. Step 3: Find a scan with type Port Scan and verify its badge. Step 4: Find a scan with type Compliance Scan and verify its badge. Step 5: Find a scan with type Full Assessment and verify its badge.
Expected Result: Each scan type has a distinct badge with correct label text: Vulnerability Scan Port Scan Compliance Scan Full Assessment.

Title: Status badges display correctly with appropriate colors
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at /scans with scans in different statuses
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /scans. Step 2: Identify a scan with queued status and verify the badge is yellow. Step 3: Identify a scan with running status and verify the badge is blue. Step 4: Identify a scan with completed status and verify the badge is green. Step 5: Verify all status badges are readable and properly colored.
Expected Result: Status badges show queued in yellow running in blue and completed in green with clear readable text.

Title: Scan row is clickable and navigates to scan detail
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /scans. Step 2: Click on any scan row in the list. Step 3: Verify the browser navigates to /scans/[id] where id is the scan identifier. Step 4: Verify the scan detail page loads showing the selected scan information. Step 5: Verify the URL in the address bar matches /scans/[id].
Expected Result: Clicking a scan row navigates to the scan detail page at /scans/[id].

Title: Stats cards show Total Scans Running Completed and Total Findings counts
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /scans. Step 2: Look for summary stats cards above the scan table. Step 3: Verify a Total Scans card shows the count of all scans. Step 4: Verify a Running card shows the count of currently running scans. Step 5: Verify a Completed card shows the count of completed scans. Step 6: Verify a Total Findings card shows the aggregate findings count.
Expected Result: Stats cards above the scan list display accurate counts for Total Scans Running Completed and Total Findings.

Title: Refresh button reloads scan data
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /scans. Step 2: Note the current scan list and counts. Step 3: Locate the Refresh button on the page. Step 4: Click the Refresh button. Step 5: Verify the scan data reloads and the list updates.
Expected Result: Refresh button triggers a data reload on the scan list page updating counts and scan statuses.

Title: New Scan button opens creation form
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /scans. Step 2: Locate the New Scan button. Step 3: Click the New Scan button. Step 4: Verify a scan creation form or modal dialog appears. Step 5: Verify the form contains fields for name type and target.
Expected Result: Clicking New Scan opens a creation form with fields for scan name scan type and target.

Title: Scan list sorted by most recent first
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at /scans with multiple scans
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /scans. Step 2: Examine the scan list order. Step 3: Verify the first scan in the list has the most recent creation date. Step 4: Verify subsequent scans have progressively older creation dates. Step 5: Create a new scan and verify it appears at the top of the list.
Expected Result: Scan list is sorted in descending order by creation date with the most recent scan at the top.

Title: Scan detail shows name type badge status badge and created timestamp
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at /scans with a completed scan
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /scans. Step 2: Click on a completed scan. Step 3: Verify the scan name is displayed prominently at the top. Step 4: Verify the scan type badge is displayed next to the name. Step 5: Verify the status badge is shown. Step 6: Verify the created timestamp is visible.
Expected Result: Scan detail page header displays the scan name type badge status badge and creation timestamp.

Title: Severity stat cards show Critical High Medium Low and Info counts
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at a completed scan detail page
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a completed scan detail at /scans/[id]. Step 2: Locate the severity stat cards section. Step 3: Verify a Critical count card is displayed with a red or dark indicator. Step 4: Verify a High count card is displayed. Step 5: Verify a Medium count card is displayed. Step 6: Verify a Low count card is displayed. Step 7: Verify an Info count card is displayed. Step 8: Verify the total of all severity counts matches the Total Findings count.
Expected Result: Five severity stat cards display counts for Critical High Medium Low and Info findings that sum to the total.

Title: Progress bar shows percentage during execution
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at a running scan detail page
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a scan that is currently running or in queued state. Step 2: Locate the progress bar element. Step 3: Verify the progress bar shows a percentage value. Step 4: Execute the scan one batch. Step 5: Verify the progress bar percentage has increased. Step 6: Verify the visual bar width corresponds to the percentage.
Expected Result: Progress bar displays the current execution percentage and updates as batches complete.

Title: Scan Information section shows targets started completed and total findings
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at a completed scan detail page
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a completed scan detail at /scans/[id]. Step 2: Locate the Scan Information section. Step 3: Verify the targets are listed. Step 4: Verify the started timestamp is shown. Step 5: Verify the completed timestamp is shown. Step 6: Verify the total findings count is displayed.
Expected Result: Scan Information section shows target list start time completion time and total findings count.

Title: Back to Scans link navigates back
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at a scan detail page
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /scans and click on a scan. Step 2: Verify the scan detail page loads. Step 3: Locate the Back to Scans link or button. Step 4: Click it. Step 5: Verify the browser navigates back to /scans. Step 6: Verify the scan list page loads correctly.
Expected Result: Back to Scans link navigates from the scan detail page back to the scan list at /scans.

Title: Execute button visible for queued or running scans
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at a queued scan detail page
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a scan with queued status. Step 2: Verify an Execute or Run Batch button is visible. Step 3: Navigate to a scan with running status. Step 4: Verify the Execute button is still visible. Step 5: Verify the button is clickable and not disabled.
Expected Result: Execute button is visible and enabled on scan detail pages for scans with queued or running status.

Title: Execute button hidden for completed scans
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at a completed scan detail page
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a scan with completed status. Step 2: Inspect the scan detail header and action buttons area. Step 3: Verify no Execute or Run Batch button is present. Step 4: Verify the completed status badge is shown. Step 5: Verify only Export and Back navigation buttons are available.
Expected Result: Execute button is not rendered on scan detail pages for scans with completed status.

Title: Export CSV button in header
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at a completed scan detail page
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a completed scan at /scans/[id]. Step 2: Locate the Export button in the scan detail header area. Step 3: Verify the Export button is visible. Step 4: Click the Export button. Step 5: Verify a CSV download initiates or an export dropdown appears.
Expected Result: Export button is visible in the scan detail header and clicking it initiates a CSV export or shows export options.

Title: Export dropdown with CSV and JSON options
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at a completed scan detail page
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a completed scan at /scans/[id]. Step 2: Locate the Export button with dropdown. Step 3: Click the dropdown arrow or trigger. Step 4: Verify CSV option is available. Step 5: Verify JSON option is available. Step 6: Select CSV and verify download. Step 7: Select JSON and verify download.
Expected Result: Export dropdown offers both CSV and JSON format options for exporting scan findings.

Title: Completed scan shows all findings in table
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at a completed scan detail page with findings
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a completed scan at /scans/[id] that has findings. Step 2: Scroll down to the findings table. Step 3: Verify the table displays all findings from the scan. Step 4: Verify the findings count in the table matches the Total Findings stat. Step 5: Verify each finding row shows at minimum the severity title and status.
Expected Result: Completed scan detail page displays a findings table containing all discovered findings with severity title and status.

Title: Execute queued scan starts first batch of 2 check modules
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with a queued full assessment scan
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a queued full assessment scan at /scans/[id]. Step 2: Verify status is queued. Step 3: Click the Execute button. Step 4: Wait for the execution to complete. Step 5: Verify the status changes to running. Step 6: Inspect the progress JSON and verify completedChecks contains 2 modules. Step 7: Verify newFindings count is returned in the response.
Expected Result: Executing a queued scan runs the first batch of 2 check modules and updates progress with completed module names.

Title: Progress updates after each batch
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with a running scan after first batch
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a running scan that has completed its first batch. Step 2: Note the current completedChecks list has 2 modules. Step 3: Click Execute again to run the next batch. Step 4: Wait for execution to complete. Step 5: Verify completedChecks now contains 4 modules. Step 6: Verify the progress percentage has increased.
Expected Result: After each execution batch the completedChecks list grows by 2 modules and the progress percentage increases.

Title: Second execute call runs next batch
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with a running scan
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a running scan at /scans/[id]. Step 2: Note the current completedChecks from the progress JSON. Step 3: Click Execute. Step 4: Wait for the batch to complete. Step 5: Verify new modules have been added to completedChecks that were not present before. Step 6: Verify the newly completed modules are different from the previous batch.
Expected Result: Each successive execute call runs the next set of check modules that have not yet been completed.

Title: Continue executing until all 8 modules complete for full scan
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with a running full assessment scan
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to the running full assessment scan. Step 2: Click Execute repeatedly until the scan completes. Step 3: After each click verify the completedChecks list grows. Step 4: After the final batch verify completedChecks contains all 8 module names. Step 5: Verify the scan status changes to completed. Step 6: Verify the progress bar shows 100 percent.
Expected Result: Full assessment scan completes after all 8 check modules have been executed across multiple batches with status changing to completed.

Title: Status transitions queued to running to completed
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with a new scan
Steps: Step 1: Create a new full assessment scan and verify status is queued. Step 2: Click Execute and verify status changes to running. Step 3: Continue executing batches. Step 4: After all modules complete verify status changes to completed. Step 5: Verify the status badge color changes from yellow to blue to green. Step 6: Refresh the page and verify the final status persists.
Expected Result: Scan status transitions correctly through queued to running to completed lifecycle stages.

Title: Each batch returns newFindings count
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com executing a scan
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a queued or running scan. Step 2: Open browser dev tools Network tab. Step 3: Click Execute. Step 4: Inspect the API response in dev tools. Step 5: Verify the response contains a newFindings field with a numeric count. Step 6: Verify the count matches the number of new findings added in that batch.
Expected Result: Each execution batch API response includes a newFindings count indicating how many findings were discovered in that batch.

Title: Progress JSON shows checkResults per module
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with a partially executed scan
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a running scan that has completed at least one batch. Step 2: Open dev tools and inspect the scan API response. Step 3: Locate the progress JSON object. Step 4: Verify completedChecks is an array of module name strings. Step 5: Verify checkResults contains per-module result data. Step 6: Verify each completed module has a corresponding entry in checkResults.
Expected Result: Progress JSON contains completedChecks array and checkResults object with per-module execution results.

Title: Execution respects scan type routing
Steps: Step 1: Create a Vulnerability Scan and execute it fully. Step 2: Verify it ran approximately 5 modules (vulnerability-specific checks). Step 3: Create a Port Scan and execute it fully. Step 4: Verify it ran approximately 2 modules (port-specific checks). Step 5: Verify different scan types route to different subsets of the 8 check modules.
Expected Result: Scan type determines which subset of the 8 check modules are executed: vulnerability runs 5 and port runs 2.

Title: Scan with unreachable target completes gracefully with 0 findings
Steps: Step 1: Create a new vulnerability scan with target nonexistent-host-12345.invalid. Step 2: Execute the scan through all batches. Step 3: Verify the scan completes without errors. Step 4: Verify the status changes to completed. Step 5: Verify the findings count is 0. Step 6: Verify no error messages or crash indicators on the page.
Expected Result: Scan targeting an unreachable host completes gracefully with 0 findings and no application errors.

Title: Execute on already-completed scan returns completed status
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with a completed scan
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a completed scan detail page. Step 2: Open dev tools console. Step 3: Execute a POST fetch to /api/scans/[id]/execute. Step 4: Verify the API response indicates the scan is already completed. Step 5: Verify no additional findings are created. Step 6: Verify the scan status remains completed.
Expected Result: Attempting to execute an already completed scan returns a completed status without re-running modules or creating duplicate findings.

Title: Findings table shows severity badge title CVE/CWE ID CVSS score and status
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at a completed scan with findings
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a completed scan detail at /scans/[id]. Step 2: Scroll to the findings table. Step 3: Verify each finding row has a severity badge with color coding. Step 4: Verify the finding title is displayed. Step 5: Verify CVE or CWE ID column is present. Step 6: Verify CVSS score is shown. Step 7: Verify status badge is displayed for each finding.
Expected Result: Findings table displays severity badge title CVE/CWE ID CVSS score and status for each finding.

Title: Findings sorted by severity with critical first
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at a completed scan with findings of mixed severity
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a completed scan detail with findings. Step 2: Examine the findings table order. Step 3: Verify critical findings appear first. Step 4: Verify high severity findings appear after critical. Step 5: Verify medium findings follow high. Step 6: Verify low and info findings are at the bottom.
Expected Result: Findings table is sorted by severity in descending order: critical then high then medium then low then info.

Title: Click expand button on finding row shows full detail
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at a completed scan with findings
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a completed scan detail. Step 2: Locate a finding row in the table. Step 3: Click the expand button or chevron on the finding row. Step 4: Verify the row expands to show additional detail. Step 5: Verify the expanded area contains more information than the summary row.
Expected Result: Clicking the expand button on a finding row reveals the full finding detail in an expanded view.

Title: Expanded view shows Description text
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with a finding expanded
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a completed scan with findings. Step 2: Expand a finding row. Step 3: Locate the Description section in the expanded view. Step 4: Verify the description text is displayed. Step 5: Verify the description provides meaningful information about the vulnerability.
Expected Result: Expanded finding view displays a Description section with text explaining the vulnerability.

Title: Expanded view shows Remediation guidance
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with a finding expanded
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a completed scan with findings. Step 2: Expand a finding row. Step 3: Locate the Remediation section in the expanded view. Step 4: Verify remediation guidance text is displayed. Step 5: Verify the guidance provides actionable steps to fix the vulnerability.
Expected Result: Expanded finding view includes a Remediation section with actionable guidance for fixing the vulnerability.

Title: Expanded view shows CVE Reference as clickable link
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with a finding that has a CVE reference
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a completed scan with findings. Step 2: Expand a finding that has a CVE ID. Step 3: Locate the CVE Reference in the expanded view. Step 4: Verify the CVE reference is displayed as a hyperlink. Step 5: Verify the link points to the appropriate CVE database URL. Step 6: Click the link and verify it opens the CVE detail page.
Expected Result: CVE references in expanded findings are rendered as clickable hyperlinks pointing to the CVE database.

Title: Expanded view shows Affected Asset
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with a finding expanded
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a completed scan with findings. Step 2: Expand a finding row. Step 3: Locate the Affected Asset section in the expanded view. Step 4: Verify the asset name or hostname is displayed. Step 5: Verify the affected asset matches the scan target.
Expected Result: Expanded finding view shows the Affected Asset name or hostname associated with the finding.

Title: Finding status badges display with correct colors
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at a completed scan with findings in various statuses
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a completed scan with findings. Step 2: Find a finding with status open and verify the badge is orange. Step 3: Find or update a finding to acknowledged and verify the badge is blue. Step 4: Find or update a finding to resolved and verify the badge is green. Step 5: Find or update a finding to false_positive and verify the badge is gray.
Expected Result: Finding status badges display with correct colors: open is orange acknowledged is blue resolved is green false_positive is gray.

Title: Acknowledge button updates finding status to acknowledged
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at a completed scan with an open finding
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a completed scan with findings. Step 2: Expand a finding with status open. Step 3: Locate the Acknowledge button. Step 4: Click the Acknowledge button. Step 5: Verify the finding status badge changes to acknowledged with blue color. Step 6: Refresh the page and verify the status persists.
Expected Result: Clicking Acknowledge updates the finding status from open to acknowledged and the change persists after page refresh.

Title: Resolve button updates finding status to resolved
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at a completed scan with an open or acknowledged finding
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a completed scan with findings. Step 2: Expand a finding that is not yet resolved. Step 3: Locate the Resolve button. Step 4: Click the Resolve button. Step 5: Verify the finding status badge changes to resolved with green color. Step 6: Refresh the page and verify the status persists.
Expected Result: Clicking Resolve updates the finding status to resolved with green badge and the change persists after refresh.

Title: False Positive button updates finding status to false_positive
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at a completed scan with an open finding
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a completed scan with findings. Step 2: Expand a finding with status open. Step 3: Locate the False Positive button. Step 4: Click the False Positive button. Step 5: Verify the finding status badge changes to false_positive with gray color. Step 6: Refresh the page and verify the status persists.
Expected Result: Clicking False Positive updates the finding status to false_positive with gray badge and the change persists after refresh.

Title: Severity filter on results API works
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with dev tools open at a completed scan
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a completed scan with findings of mixed severity. Step 2: Open dev tools console. Step 3: Execute a fetch to /api/scans/[id]/results?severity=critical. Step 4: Verify only critical severity findings are returned. Step 5: Execute a fetch with ?severity=high. Step 6: Verify only high severity findings are returned.
Expected Result: The severity query parameter on the scan results API correctly filters findings to only the specified severity level.

Title: Export CSV downloads file with correct headers
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at a completed scan with findings
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a completed scan at /scans/[id]. Step 2: Click the Export CSV button. Step 3: Verify a file download initiates. Step 4: Open the downloaded CSV file. Step 5: Verify the header row contains: ID Severity Title CVE ID CVSS Score Status Description Remediation Asset Asset IP Created At. Step 6: Verify the file extension is .csv.
Expected Result: CSV export downloads a file with correct column headers: ID Severity Title CVE ID CVSS Score Status Description Remediation Asset Asset IP Created At.

Title: CSV export contains all findings data
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at a completed scan with findings
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a completed scan with known findings count. Step 2: Click Export CSV. Step 3: Open the downloaded CSV file. Step 4: Count the data rows (excluding header). Step 5: Verify the row count matches the Total Findings count on the scan detail page. Step 6: Verify each row has populated fields for severity title and status.
Expected Result: Exported CSV contains one data row per finding matching the total findings count with all fields populated.

Title: Export JSON returns structured data with scan metadata and findings array
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at a completed scan with findings
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a completed scan at /scans/[id]. Step 2: Click the Export dropdown and select JSON. Step 3: Verify a JSON file downloads. Step 4: Open the JSON file. Step 5: Verify the root object contains scan metadata (name type status). Step 6: Verify a findings array is present containing all finding objects.
Expected Result: JSON export produces a structured file with scan metadata at the root level and a findings array containing all finding objects.

Title: JSON export includes exportedAt timestamp
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at a completed scan
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a completed scan. Step 2: Click Export JSON. Step 3: Open the downloaded JSON file. Step 4: Locate the exportedAt field. Step 5: Verify it contains a valid ISO 8601 timestamp. Step 6: Verify the timestamp is close to the current time.
Expected Result: JSON export includes an exportedAt field with a valid ISO timestamp indicating when the export was generated.

Title: Export requires scan.export capability
Preconditions: Logged in as viewer@exargen.com with password Viewer123!
Steps: Step 1: Log in as viewer@exargen.com. Step 2: Open dev tools console. Step 3: Execute a GET fetch to /api/scans/[id]/results/export. Step 4: Verify the response is 403 Forbidden. Step 5: Log in as analyst@exargen.com with password Analyst123!. Step 6: Execute the same export API call. Step 7: Verify the response is 200 OK with export data.
Expected Result: Export API requires scan.export capability. Viewer gets 403 while analyst with scan.export gets 200.

Title: Export with no findings returns empty results
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with a completed scan that has 0 findings
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to a completed scan that has 0 findings. Step 2: Click Export CSV. Step 3: Verify the CSV file downloads with only the header row and no data rows. Step 4: Click Export JSON. Step 5: Verify the JSON file has an empty findings array. Step 6: Verify no errors occur during export.
Expected Result: Exporting a scan with no findings produces valid files with headers but no data rows for CSV and an empty findings array for JSON.

Title: Asset auto-created when scanning new target hostname
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /assets and count existing assets. Step 2: Navigate to /scans. Step 3: Create a new scan with a target hostname not matching any existing asset such as unique-scanner-test-target.example.com. Step 4: Navigate to /assets. Step 5: Verify the asset count increased by one. Step 6: Find the newly created asset matching the scan target hostname. Step 7: Verify the asset has correct name and type.
Expected Result: Scanning a new target hostname that does not exist as an asset automatically creates a new asset record with that hostname.

Title: Asset detail page shows name type criticality and status
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at /assets with a scanner-created asset
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /assets. Step 2: Click on a scanner-created asset to open /assets/[id]. Step 3: Verify the asset name is displayed. Step 4: Verify the asset type is shown. Step 5: Verify the criticality level is displayed. Step 6: Verify the status indicator is visible.
Expected Result: Asset detail page at /assets/[id] shows the asset name type criticality level and operational status.

Title: Asset detail shows Risk Score Open Findings Last Scanned and IP Address
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at an asset detail page with scan findings
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /assets/[id] for an asset that has been scanned. Step 2: Verify the Risk Score metric is displayed. Step 3: Verify the Open Findings count is shown. Step 4: Verify the Last Scanned timestamp is present. Step 5: Verify the IP Address field is displayed.
Expected Result: Asset detail page displays Risk Score Open Findings Last Scanned timestamp and IP Address metrics.

Title: Asset detail shows severity breakdown counts
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at an asset detail page with findings
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /assets/[id] for an asset with scan findings. Step 2: Locate the severity breakdown section. Step 3: Verify critical count is displayed. Step 4: Verify high count is displayed. Step 5: Verify medium count is displayed. Step 6: Verify low count is displayed. Step 7: Verify info count is displayed.
Expected Result: Asset detail page shows a severity breakdown with separate counts for critical high medium low and info findings.

Title: Asset detail shows related findings from all scans
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at an asset detail page
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /assets/[id] for an asset that was scanned in multiple scans. Step 2: Scroll to the findings section on the asset detail page. Step 3: Verify findings from all related scans are displayed. Step 4: Verify each finding shows its source scan reference. Step 5: Verify the findings count matches the total from all scans targeting this asset.
Expected Result: Asset detail page aggregates and displays related findings from all scans that targeted this asset.

Title: Asset detail shows tags
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at an asset detail page
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /assets/[id]. Step 2: Locate the tags section on the asset detail page. Step 3: Verify tags are displayed as badges or chips. Step 4: Verify auto-applied tags from scanner are present if applicable. Step 5: Verify the tag display matches the tags assigned to this asset.
Expected Result: Asset detail page displays all tags associated with the asset as visual badges.

Title: Asset list row is clickable and navigates to asset detail
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /assets. Step 2: Verify assets are displayed in a list or table. Step 3: Click on an asset row. Step 4: Verify the browser navigates to /assets/[id]. Step 5: Verify the asset detail page loads with the correct asset information.
Expected Result: Clicking an asset row in the asset list navigates to the asset detail page at /assets/[id].

Title: Back to Assets link on detail page
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /assets and click on an asset. Step 2: Verify the asset detail page loads. Step 3: Locate the Back to Assets link or button. Step 4: Click it. Step 5: Verify the browser navigates back to /assets. Step 6: Verify the asset list page loads correctly.
Expected Result: Back to Assets link on the asset detail page navigates back to the asset list at /assets.

Title: Dashboard Total Assets count includes scanner-created assets
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /dashboard. Step 2: Note the Total Assets count. Step 3: Navigate to /scans and create a new scan with a new unique target hostname. Step 4: Navigate back to /dashboard. Step 5: Verify the Total Assets count has increased by one. Step 6: Verify the count matches the total in /assets.
Expected Result: Dashboard Total Assets KPI accurately reflects all assets including those auto-created by the vulnerability scanner.

Title: Dashboard Critical Vulnerabilities count reflects scan findings
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at /dashboard after scans with critical findings
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /dashboard. Step 2: Locate the Critical Vulnerabilities count widget. Step 3: Note the current count. Step 4: Navigate to /scans and verify completed scans have critical findings. Step 5: Compare the dashboard count with the sum of critical findings across all scans. Step 6: Verify the counts match.
Expected Result: Dashboard Critical Vulnerabilities count accurately reflects the total number of critical severity findings from all scans.

Title: Dashboard Risk Score calculated from severity counts
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /dashboard. Step 2: Locate the Risk Score widget. Step 3: Verify a numeric risk score is displayed. Step 4: Navigate to /scans and review findings by severity. Step 5: Verify the dashboard risk score correlates with the weighted severity of scan findings. Step 6: Verify higher critical and high counts result in a higher risk score.
Expected Result: Dashboard Risk Score is calculated based on the severity distribution of scan findings with higher weight for critical and high severities.

Title: Dashboard Vulnerability Breakdown chart shows finding distribution
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /dashboard. Step 2: Locate the Vulnerability Breakdown chart or widget. Step 3: Verify the chart shows a distribution of findings by severity. Step 4: Verify critical high medium low and info segments are represented. Step 5: Verify the chart values match the actual finding counts from scans.
Expected Result: Dashboard Vulnerability Breakdown chart accurately displays the distribution of findings across severity levels.

Title: Dashboard Total Findings matches scan results
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /dashboard. Step 2: Locate the Total Findings count. Step 3: Note the number. Step 4: Navigate to /scans. Step 5: Sum up the findings counts from all completed scans. Step 6: Verify the dashboard Total Findings matches the aggregated sum from scans.
Expected Result: Dashboard Total Findings count equals the sum of all findings across all completed scans.

Title: Dashboard Recent Activity shows scan-related audit entries
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /dashboard. Step 2: Locate the Recent Activity or Activity Feed section. Step 3: Look for entries related to scan creation or scan execution. Step 4: Verify scan-related audit events appear in the activity feed. Step 5: Verify entries show the action timestamp and actor.
Expected Result: Dashboard Recent Activity section includes audit entries for scan creation and execution events.

Title: SIEM events page shows scanner-generated events
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /siem. Step 3: Look for events with source or type related to the vulnerability scanner. Step 4: Verify scanner-generated SIEM events are present in the list. Step 5: Verify events include relevant details such as scan name or finding severity.
Expected Result: SIEM events page displays events that were generated by the vulnerability scanner during scan execution.

Title: SIEM alerts include critical finding alerts
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at /siem with scans that produced critical findings
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /siem. Step 2: Filter or search for alert-type events. Step 3: Verify alerts exist for critical severity findings from scans. Step 4: Verify alert details reference the specific critical vulnerability. Step 5: Verify the alert severity matches the finding severity.
Expected Result: SIEM alerts are generated for critical severity scan findings with appropriate severity and vulnerability details.

Title: Notification bell badge count matches open alerts
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at any page
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to any page with the top navigation bar visible. Step 2: Locate the notification bell icon. Step 3: Verify a badge count is displayed on the bell. Step 4: Navigate to /siem and count the open unacknowledged alerts. Step 5: Verify the bell badge count matches the number of open alerts.
Expected Result: Notification bell badge displays a count that matches the number of open unacknowledged SIEM alerts.

Title: Notification bell dropdown shows alert titles and severity
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at any page
Steps: Step 1: Locate the notification bell icon in the top navigation. Step 2: Click the bell to open the dropdown. Step 3: Verify the dropdown shows a list of recent alerts. Step 4: Verify each alert entry shows a title. Step 5: Verify each alert entry shows a severity indicator or badge. Step 6: Verify the alerts are ordered by most recent first.
Expected Result: Notification bell dropdown displays alert entries with titles and severity indicators ordered by most recent first.

Title: View all in SIEM link navigates to /siem
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com with notification bell dropdown open
Steps: Step 1: Click the notification bell to open the dropdown. Step 2: Look for a View all or View all in SIEM link at the bottom of the dropdown. Step 3: Click the link. Step 4: Verify the browser navigates to /siem. Step 5: Verify the SIEM events page loads correctly.
Expected Result: View all in SIEM link in the notification bell dropdown navigates to the full SIEM events page at /siem.

Title: AI Actions page shows scanner-generated remediation suggestions
Steps: Step 1: Log in as admin@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /ai-actions. Step 3: Look for AI actions that were generated by the vulnerability scanner. Step 4: Verify remediation suggestion actions are present. Step 5: Verify the actions reference specific scan findings or vulnerabilities.
Expected Result: AI Actions page displays remediation suggestions that were automatically generated from vulnerability scan findings.

Title: AI action shows type risk level and status pending
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at /ai-actions with scanner-generated actions
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /ai-actions. Step 2: Find a scanner-generated AI action. Step 3: Verify the action type is displayed. Step 4: Verify the risk level indicator is shown. Step 5: Verify the status shows pending for unapproved actions.
Expected Result: Scanner-generated AI actions display their type risk level and pending status correctly on the AI Actions page.

Title: Approve pending AI action changes status to approved
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at /ai-actions with a pending action
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /ai-actions. Step 2: Find a pending AI action. Step 3: Click the Approve button. Step 4: Verify the action status changes to approved. Step 5: Refresh the page. Step 6: Verify the approved status persists.
Expected Result: Approving a pending AI action updates its status to approved and the change persists after page refresh.

Title: Reject pending AI action changes status to rejected
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at /ai-actions with a pending action
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /ai-actions. Step 2: Find a pending AI action. Step 3: Click the Reject button. Step 4: Verify the action status changes to rejected. Step 5: Refresh the page. Step 6: Verify the rejected status persists.
Expected Result: Rejecting a pending AI action updates its status to rejected and the change persists after page refresh.

Title: AI action references scan ID and target in config
Preconditions: Logged in as admin@exargen.com at /ai-actions with scanner-generated actions
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /ai-actions. Step 2: Find a scanner-generated AI action. Step 3: Click to view the action details or expand it. Step 4: Locate the configuration section. Step 5: Verify the config contains a reference to the scan ID. Step 6: Verify the config contains the target hostname from the scan.
Expected Result: Scanner-generated AI action configuration includes references to the originating scan ID and target hostname.

Title: scan.view: viewer CAN see scan list but CANNOT create or execute
Preconditions: Logged in as viewer@exargen.com with password Viewer123!
Steps: Step 1: Log in as viewer@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /scans. Step 3: Verify the scan list page loads if viewer has scan.view or shows access denied if not. Step 4: Verify no New Scan button is visible. Step 5: Click on a scan to view detail. Step 6: Verify no Execute button is visible on the detail page.
Expected Result: Viewer with scan.view can see the scan list and detail but no creation or execution controls are available.

Title: scan.create: analyst CAN create scans
Preconditions: Logged in as analyst@exargen.com with password Analyst123!
Steps: Step 1: Log in as analyst@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /scans. Step 3: Verify the New Scan button is visible. Step 4: Click New Scan. Step 5: Fill in scan details with name type and target. Step 6: Click Create Scan. Step 7: Verify the scan is created successfully and appears in the list.
Expected Result: Analyst with scan.create capability can access the New Scan button and successfully create scans.

Title: scan.execute: required for Execute button and API
Preconditions: Logged in as auditor@exargen.com with password Auditor123!
Steps: Step 1: Log in as auditor@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /scans and click on a queued scan. Step 3: Verify no Execute button is visible (auditor lacks scan.execute). Step 4: Open dev tools console. Step 5: Execute a POST fetch to /api/scans/[id]/execute. Step 6: Verify the response is 403 Forbidden.
Expected Result: Execute button is hidden and the execute API returns 403 for users without scan.execute capability.

Title: scan.export: required for Export buttons
Preconditions: Logged in as viewer@exargen.com with password Viewer123!
Steps: Step 1: Log in as viewer@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to a completed scan detail page. Step 3: Verify no Export button or dropdown is visible. Step 4: Open dev tools console. Step 5: Execute a GET fetch to /api/scans/[id]/results/export. Step 6: Verify the response is 403 Forbidden.
Expected Result: Export buttons are hidden and export API returns 403 for users without scan.export capability.

Title: Auditor can view scans but not create or execute
Preconditions: Logged in as auditor@exargen.com with password Auditor123!
Steps: Step 1: Log in as auditor@exargen.com. Step 2: Navigate to /scans. Step 3: Verify the scan list loads and scans are visible (scan.view). Step 4: Verify no New Scan button is present (no scan.create). Step 5: Click on a scan detail. Step 6: Verify no Execute button is present (no scan.execute). Step 7: Verify Export button IS visible (auditor has scan.export).
Expected Result: Auditor can view scans and export results but cannot create new scans or execute existing ones.

Title: Scan with private IP completes gracefully with 0 findings
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /scans. Step 2: Create a new vulnerability scan with target 192.168.1.1. Step 3: Execute the scan through all batches. Step 4: Verify the scan completes without crashing. Step 5: Verify status becomes completed. Step 6: Verify findings count is 0 since Vercel cannot reach private IPs.
Expected Result: Scanning a private IP address completes gracefully with 0 findings and no application errors since the target is unreachable from Vercel.

Title: Scan with invalid hostname completes gracefully
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /scans. Step 2: Create a new scan with target !!!invalid-hostname!!!. Step 3: Execute the scan. Step 4: Verify the scan completes without throwing unhandled errors. Step 5: Verify status transitions to completed. Step 6: Verify the UI does not show a crash or error page.
Expected Result: Scanning an invalid hostname completes gracefully with the scan reaching completed status and 0 findings without any UI errors.

Title: Multiple concurrent scans can exist
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /scans. Step 2: Create scan A with target-a.example.com. Step 3: Create scan B with target-b.example.com. Step 4: Create scan C with target-c.example.com. Step 5: Verify all three scans appear in the scan list. Step 6: Execute scan A and verify it does not affect scan B or C status.
Expected Result: Multiple scans can exist simultaneously in different states without interfering with each other.

Title: Long hostname or URL handling with no overflow in UI
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /scans. Step 2: Create a new scan with a very long target hostname such as this-is-a-very-long-subdomain-name-for-testing-overflow-behavior.deeply.nested.subdomain.example.com. Step 3: Verify the scan is created. Step 4: Navigate to the scan list. Step 5: Verify the long hostname does not overflow or break the table layout. Step 6: Click into the scan detail and verify the target is fully visible or properly truncated.
Expected Result: Long hostnames are handled gracefully in the UI with proper text wrapping or truncation and no layout overflow.

Title: Back navigation from scan detail to scan list preserves state
Steps: Step 1: Navigate to /scans. Step 2: Note the current scan list and any filters applied. Step 3: Click on a scan to view its detail at /scans/[id]. Step 4: Review the scan detail page. Step 5: Click the Back to Scans link or use browser back button. Step 6: Verify the scan list page loads with the same state as before navigation.
Expected Result: Navigating back from scan detail to scan list preserves the previous page state and list ordering.
